Identifier of the user who closed and finalized this food cost period. Typically a restaurant manager or accounting staff member.
Identifier of the restaurant unit for which this food cost period is calculated. Links to the specific restaurant location.
The actual Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the period, calculated as: Opening Inventory + Purchases + Transfers In - Transfers Out - Closing Inventory. The real food cost incurred.
reference: GAAP Cost of Goods Sold Standards

description: Any manual adjustments applied to the food cost calculation for the period, such as corrections for inventory errors, vendor credits, or accounting reclassifications. Positive or negative value.
Explanation for any manual adjustment applied to the food cost calculation. Provides audit trail and justification for period adjustments.

The total beverage sales revenue for the period. Tracked separately from food sales for category-specific cost analysis.
The date and time when this food cost period was officially closed and calculations were finalized. Marks the completion of the period-close process.
The total dollar value of food inventory on hand at the end of the period, determined by physical count or perpetual inventory system. Represents the ending stock value for COGS calculation.

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Percentage Actual
description: The actual food cost as a percentage of food sales revenue for the period. Key performance metric for restaurant profitability and cost control. Calculated as (Actual Food Cost / Food Sales Revenue) * 100.

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Percentage Theoretical
description: The theoretical food cost as a percentage of food sales revenue for the period. Represents the ideal COGS% if all operational standards were met. Calculated as (Theoretical Food Cost / Food Sales Revenue) * 100.
The method used to determine closing inventory value: physical (manual count), perpetual (system-tracked), or hybrid (system with periodic physical verification).
The date and time when this food cost period record was first created in the system. Marks the initiation of the period tracking.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for international restaurant chains.
The total food sales revenue (excluding beverage) for the period. Used as the denominator in COGS% calculations. Sourced from Point of Sale (POS) system.
The date and time when this food cost period record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.

Free-text notes or comments about this food cost period, such as unusual events, operational issues, inventory discrepancies, or explanations for variance. Used for management review and audit purposes.
The total dollar value of food inventory on hand at the beginning of the period. Represents the starting stock value for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation.
The last date of the accounting period for which food costs are being calculated. Marks the close of the cost accumulation window.
The accounting period identifier in format YYYY-PNN (e.g., 2024-P01 for period 1 of 2024). Used for financial reporting alignment.
The first date of the accounting period for which food costs are being calculated. Marks the beginning of the cost accumulation window.

The current status of the food cost period: open (still accepting transactions), closed (period ended, calculations complete), locked (finalized, no further changes allowed), or under_review (being audited or reviewed).
The frequency type of the accounting period for food cost calculation (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, or quarterly).
The date on which the physical inventory count was performed to determine closing inventory value. Typically the last day of the period or the first day of the next period.
The total dollar value of all food and beverage purchases received during the period. Includes all vendor invoices posted to inventory during the period.
The calculated ideal food cost based on recipe costing and actual sales mix (PMIX). Represents what food cost should have been if all recipes were followed perfectly with no waste or theft.
The combined food and beverage sales revenue for the period. Represents total top-line revenue for the restaurant unit during the period.
The total dollar value of inventory transferred into this restaurant unit from other locations during the period. Increases available inventory.
The total dollar value of inventory transferred out of this restaurant unit to other locations during the period. Decreases available inventory.

The dollar difference between actual food cost and theoretical food cost (Actual - Theoretical). Positive variance indicates higher-than-expected costs due to waste, theft, portioning errors, or recipe non-compliance.
The variance expressed as a percentage of theoretical food cost. Calculated as (Variance Amount / Theoretical Food Cost) * 100. Used to assess the magnitude of cost control issues.
The waste value as a percentage of total food usage (Waste Value / (Actual Food Cost + Waste Value)) * 100. Key metric for food quality control and operational efficiency.

The total dollar value of food waste recorded during the period, including spoilage, expiration, preparation errors, and quality control discards. Tracked for Waste% calculation and food cost control.

business_glossary_term: Impacts Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Flag

Additional free-text notes or comments providing context, details, or special instructions related to the inventory adjustment.

The on-hand inventory quantity for this stock item at this location immediately before the adjustment was applied.

description: The cost per unit of the stock item at the time of adjustment, used to calculate the total adjustment value.

Reference to the parent category in the hierarchical taxonomy. Null for top-level categories. Enables drill-down reporting from major groups to item classes.
Self-referencing FK on item_category (parent_item_category_id)
Identifier of the user who created this category record. Used for audit trail and data governance.
Date when this category was activated and became available for use. Used for historical analysis and category lifecycle tracking.

description: Detailed description of the category scope, including what types of items are classified under this category and any special handling or procurement considerations.

High‑level classification of the category for analytics and cost allocation.

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Budget Target Percentage

Default cost center code for expense allocation for items in this category. Used for departmental cost tracking and variance analysis.
Timestamp when this category record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Standard frequency in days for cycle counting items in this category. High-value or high-risk categories have shorter frequencies. Used for inventory accuracy programs.
Date when this category was deactivated and removed from active use. Null for active categories. Used for historical reporting and compliance.
Business reason for deactivating this category. Examples: consolidated into another category, no longer used, taxonomy restructure. Null for active categories.
Target gross margin percentage applied to items in this category.

business_glossary_term: Default Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) Level Days
description: Standard PAR level in days of inventory for items in this category. Used to calculate reorder points and optimize stock levels. Varies by commodity type and lead time.

description: Typical periodic automatic replenishment quantity for the category.

Standard sales tax rate applicable to the category.
Standard unit of measure for items in this category. Examples: LB (pounds), EA (each), CS (case), GAL (gallon), KG (kilogram). Used as default for new item setup.
Expected waste percentage for the category used in cost modeling.
Top-level division to which this category belongs. Used for high-level financial reporting and procurement segmentation.
Date when the category becomes valid for use.
Date when the category is retired; null if still active.

Indicates whether HACCP monitoring is mandatory for items in this category.
Depth of the category in the hierarchy (root = 0).
Indicates whether this category is currently active and available for use. Inactive categories are retained for historical reporting but cannot be assigned to new items.
Indicates whether items in this category are perishable and require temperature control.
Detailed textual description of the category purpose and scope.
Current lifecycle status of the category.
Timestamp when this category record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and incremental data processing.
Timestamp of the most recent governance review of the category definition.
Indicates whether items in this category can be sourced from multiple vendors. False for categories requiring single-source consistency (e.g., branded ingredients, proprietary items).

description: Indicates whether items in this category require temperature-controlled storage and handling per HACCP guidelines. True for perishable, refrigerated, and frozen categories.

Display sequence number for this category within its parent level. Used for consistent ordering in reports, menus, and user interfaces.
Originating system that supplied the category record (e.g., "MarketMan").
Tax classification code for items in this category. Used for sales tax calculation and compliance reporting. Examples: FOOD (tax-exempt food), TAXABLE (standard rate), BEVERAGE (special rate).

Most common waste reason for this category.
Average usable yield percentage for the category during preparation.

description: Unique identifier for the unit of measure. Primary key.
Reference to the base UOM for this measurement type. Used to establish conversion chains. Null if this UOM is itself the base unit.

Indicates whether items measured in this UOM require Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) temperature monitoring during storage and transfer. Supports food safety compliance per Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations.

business_glossary_term: Conversion Factor to Base Unit of Measure (UOM)
description: Multiplier to convert this UOM to the base UOM (e.g., 1 pound = 453.592 grams, so conversion_factor_to_base = 453.592). Critical for accurate Cost of Goods Sold (COGS%) calculation and yield management across ordering, storage, and recipe contexts.
Date and time when this UOM record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage tracking per Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) requirements.

Default shelf life in days for items measured in this UOM. Used as a fallback when Stock Keeping Unit (SKU)-specific shelf life is not defined. Supports waste tracking (Waste%) and First In First Out (FIFO) rotation.
Sort order for displaying UOMs in user interfaces and dropdown lists. Lower numbers appear first. Supports user experience optimization in Point of Sale (POS) and inventory management systems.
Date when this UOM was retired or deprecated. Null for currently active UOMs. Supports historical reporting and audit trails for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS%) analysis.
Date when this UOM became available for use in inventory transactions. Supports temporal validity and historical analysis of UOM changes.

business_glossary_term: Is Base Unit of Measure (UOM) Flag
description: Indicates whether this UOM is the base unit for its type (e.g., gram for weight, milliliter for volume). Base UOMs have a conversion factor of 1.0 and serve as the reference for all conversions within their type.
Indicates whether this UOM is a system-defined standard (e.g., gram, liter, each) that cannot be modified or deleted by users. Protects core reference data integrity.
ISO 80000 standard code for the unit of measure. Supports international operations and regulatory compliance across multiple jurisdictions.
Date and time when this UOM record was last updated. Supports change tracking and data governance for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS%) accuracy and regulatory compliance.
Free-text field for additional context, usage guidelines, or special handling instructions for this UOM. Supports operational documentation and training materials.
Indicates whether this UOM is used in purchase orders and vendor ordering (e.g., case, pallet). Supports procurement workflows and vendor catalog integration.
Plural form of the UOM name (e.g., Pounds, Ounces, Cases). Supports grammatically correct reporting and user interface text generation.
Number of decimal places to use when displaying or calculating quantities in this UOM. Ensures consistent rounding and precision across inventory transactions.
Indicates whether this UOM is used in recipe Bill of Materials (BOM) and menu item costing (e.g., ounce, gram, tablespoon). Critical for accurate recipe yield management and menu engineering.
Indicates whether items measured in this UOM require lot number and expiration date tracking for traceability. Supports food safety recalls and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Needed for loyalty points accrual and member order history report linking each order to the member who placed it.

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.marketing_guest_segment. Business justification: Supports segment‑based sales analysis: orders are associated with the guest segment targeted by marketing campaigns for segmentation reporting.

Foreign key linking to menu.menu. Business justification: Required for Menu Performance Reporting: each order must be linked to the specific menu version presented to the guest to analyze sales per menu and ensure compliance with menu engineering.

Identifier of the guest (customer) who placed the order. Nullable for anonymous walk-in transactions where no loyalty or digital profile is associated. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction header.

Identifier of the marketing promotion, coupon, or Limited Time Offer (LTO) applied to this order. Null if no promotion was applied. Used for promotional campaign effectiveness and discount attribution analysis.

description: Indicates whether the order contains at least one Limited Time Offer (LTO) menu item. Used for LTO performance tracking, promotional sales attribution, and menu engineering analysis.

description: Indicates whether the order was voided by a manager after tender. Voided orders are retained for audit and loss prevention purposes but excluded from revenue reporting. Distinct from cancelled orders (cancelled before preparation).

description: Total number of individual menu items (including modifiers and add-ons) included in the order. Used for throughput analysis, kitchen capacity planning, and Average Transaction Count (ATC) benchmarking.

description: Timestamp when the order was routed to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for preparation. Used to calculate kitchen queue time and Back of House (BOH) throughput metrics.

description: Number of loyalty reward points earned by the guest for this order transaction. Used for loyalty program accrual tracking and guest engagement analytics.
Number of loyalty reward points redeemed by the guest as part of this order transaction. Used for loyalty liability accounting and promotional cost analysis.
External order reference number assigned by the Olo Digital Ordering Platform for orders placed through digital channels (web, mobile app). Null for in-store POS orders.

Current lifecycle state of the guest order. Tracks the order from placement through fulfillment or cancellation. 'placed' = order received; 'in_preparation' = KDS routing active; 'ready' = food ready for pickup/delivery; 'fulfilled' = order handed to guest; 'cancelled' = cancelled before preparation; 'voided' = voided post-tender by manager.

description: Fulfillment type of the order indicating how the guest intends to receive their food. Distinct from order_channel (which captures the ordering interface). Used for kitchen routing, packaging, and service time analytics.

description: Number of guests in the dining party associated with this order. Applicable for dine-in orders. Used for cover count reporting, table turn analysis, and per-cover revenue calculations.

description: Current status of the payment transaction associated with this order. Distinct from order_status which tracks fulfillment lifecycle. Used for financial reconciliation and exception management.

description: Native transaction reference or check number generated by the Oracle MICROS POS system. Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse and the POS system of record.

description: Total elapsed time in seconds from order placement (placed_at) to order fulfillment (fulfilled_at). Represents the end-to-end guest experience time. A primary operational KPI for Quick-Service Restaurants (QSR) and Drive-Thru (DT) performance benchmarking.

description: Originating operational system of record from which this order record was ingested into the lakehouse. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-channel integration auditing.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Data Architecture Standard

Table or seat identifier assigned to the dine-in order within the restaurant floor plan. Null for takeout, drive-thru, and delivery orders. Used for table turn analysis and Front of House (FOH) service management.

description: Total sales tax amount applied to the order based on applicable local, state, and federal tax rates. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment. Used for tax remittance reporting and financial reconciliation.

Elapsed time in seconds from order placement (placed_at) to order ready (ready_at). Represents the kitchen preparation time, a key Speed of Service (SOS) and throughput KPI in restaurant operations. Stored as a raw operational field captured by the POS/KDS system.

description: Gratuity amount added by the guest at the time of payment. Applicable for casual and fine-dining service models. Used for labor compensation reporting and financial reconciliation.

reference: GAAP/IFRS; Oracle MICROS POS; PCI DSS

description: Timestamp when this guest_order record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this transaction header. Used for incremental data processing and change data capture (CDC) pipelines.
Reason code for why the order was voided. Null for non-voided orders. Used for loss prevention, operational exception reporting, and manager accountability tracking.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each line item within a guest order. Primary key for the order_item entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

description: Reference to the combo meal or bundle to which this line item belongs. Null when is_combo_component is False. Groups combo components for bundle-level revenue and PMIX reporting.

description: Reference to the combo meal or bundle to which this line item belongs. Null when is_combo_component is False. Groups combo components for bundle-level revenue and PMIX reporting.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Combo Meal Record

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.contract_line. Business justification: COGS attribution report requires linking each sold menu item to the contract line that supplies its primary ingredient for cost and compliance tracking.

description: Reference to the parent guest order (TRANSACTION_HEADER) to which this line item belongs. Establishes the header/detail relationship for order fulfillment and revenue reporting.

Reference to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) station to which this line item was routed for preparation. Supports kitchen throughput and ticket time analysis.

description: Reference to the menu item ordered, linking to the menu domain for Product Mix (PMIX) analysis, recipe costing, and allergen reporting.

Reference to the modifier applied to this line item (e.g., extra cheese, no onions, upsize). Nullable when no modifier is applied. Supports modifier-level PMIX and upsell analysis.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this line item (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global restaurant markets.

description: Indicates whether this line item is a component of a combo meal or bundle (True) or a standalone item (False). Supports combo meal PMIX analysis and bundle pricing validation.

description: Reference to the restaurant location where this modifier was applied. Enables location-level modifier adoption analysis, Product Mix (PMIX) reporting, and kitchen execution fidelity tracking.

description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this tax line (e.g., 'USD', 'CAD', 'GBP'). Required for multi-currency operations and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.
The meal period or daypart during which the order was placed (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, snack). Used for daypart-level tax and revenue analysis in foodservice operations.
reference: NRA Daypart Reporting Standards; Oracle MICROS POS Daypart Configuration

description: Identifies the system or engine that calculated and applied this tax line (e.g., pos = Oracle MICROS POS built-in tax, vertex = Vertex O Series, avalara = Avalara AvaTax, sap_tax = SAP S/4HANA tax engine, manual = manually entered). Used for reconciliation and audit.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Tax Configuration; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; Vertex O Series

description: Reference number or identifier of the tax exemption certificate presented by the guest or organization. Required for audit documentation when is_exempt is True. Null when no exemption applies.
reference: IRS Publication 557; State Revenue Authority Exemption Certificate Requirements

description: The reason code for a tax exemption when is_exempt is True (e.g., government, non_profit, resale, diplomatic, employee_meal, other). Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance. Null when is_exempt is False.
reference: IRS Tax Exemption Guidelines; State Revenue Codes; FTC Franchise Disclosure Rules

description: Indicates whether this tax line was flagged as exempt (True) due to a guest exemption certificate, government order, or qualifying non-profit status. When True, tax_amount should be zero and exemption_reason should be populated.
reference: IRS Tax Exemption Guidelines; State Revenue Codes; USDA Exemption Rules

description: Indicates whether the tax amount on this line has been refunded to the guest (True) as part of an order refund or cancellation. Used for refund reconciliation and tax reversal tracking.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Refund Processing; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Reversal; GAAP ASC 606

description: Indicates whether this tax was already included in the menu price (True) or added at checkout on top of the menu price (False). Critical for VAT/GST jurisdictions and for accurate revenue recognition and tax remittance calculations.

Sequential number ordering multiple tax lines within the same order (e.g., 1 = state sales tax, 2 = county tax, 3 = city beverage tax). Ensures deterministic ordering for receipt display and financial reporting.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Tax Line Sequencing; NRA Receipt Standards

description: The service channel through which the order was placed (pos = in-store Point of Sale, drive_thru = Drive-Thru (DT), olo = Online Ordering (OLO), third_party_delivery = Third-Party Delivery (3PD), kiosk = self-service kiosk, catering). Used for channel-level tax analysis and compliance.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; NRA Channel Reporting Standards

description: The tax amount as originally computed at the time of order placement, before any post-transaction adjustments. Populated when tax_status = 'adjusted' to preserve the audit trail of the original calculation.
reference: GAAP ASC 740; IFRS IAS 12; IRS Audit Requirements

description: The business date (yyyy-MM-dd) to which this tax line is attributed for tax remittance and financial period reporting. May differ from the transaction timestamp for end-of-day batch processing or period adjustments.
reference: GAAP ASC 740; IFRS IAS 12; IRS Tax Period Rules

business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Tax Line Reference
description: The native tax line identifier or sequence number assigned by Oracle MICROS POS for this tax entry. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the POS transaction log and the lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Tax Transaction Log

description: The applicable tax rate expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.0875 for 8.75%). Sourced from the tax engine or POS tax configuration at the time of the transaction. Used for tax amount validation and audit.
reference: State and Local Tax Authority Rate Tables; SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition Records

description: The tax amount refunded to the guest when is_refunded is True. May be a partial refund (less than tax_amount) for partial order refunds. Used for financial reconciliation and tax reversal reporting.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Refund Processing; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; GAAP ASC 606

description: The fiscal period (e.g., '2024-Q1', '2024-03') in which this tax amount is scheduled for remittance to the tax authority. Used for tax filing calendar management and period-end close.
reference: IRS Tax Filing Calendar; State Revenue Authority Filing Schedules; GAAP ASC 740

description: Indicates whether this tax amount has been remitted to the applicable tax authority (pending = not yet remitted, remitted = successfully paid to authority, not_applicable = exempt or zero-rate, failed = remittance attempt failed). Supports tax compliance tracking.
reference: IRS Tax Remittance Requirements; State Revenue Authority Filing Rules; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX

description: The SAP S/4HANA FI-TX tax document number associated with this tax line for GL posting and tax remittance. Enables end-to-end traceability from POS transaction to ERP financial posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Document; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting Standards

description: The operational system of record from which this tax line record originated (micros_pos = Oracle MICROS POS, sap_s4hana = SAP S/4HANA FI-TX, olo = Olo Digital Ordering Platform, manual = manually entered). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; SAP S/4HANA; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Data Lineage Standards

description: Two-letter US state code (or equivalent provincial code) of the tax jurisdiction where the restaurant is located (e.g., 'IL', 'CA', 'TX'). Used for state-level tax rate lookup, remittance routing, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ANSI INCITS 38; IRS State Tax Authority Codes; State Revenue Departments

description: The internal or POS-assigned tax code identifying the specific tax rule applied (e.g., 'TX_CA_SALES', 'TX_CHI_BEV'). Maps to the tax condition record in SAP S/4HANA and Oracle MICROS POS tax configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition Records; Oracle MICROS POS Tax Configuration

description: Human-readable name of the tax as displayed on the guest receipt or financial report (e.g., 'Illinois Sales Tax', 'Chicago Beverage Tax', 'CA Bag Fee'). Required for receipt compliance and guest transparency.
reference: NRA Receipt Compliance Guidelines; State Consumer Protection Laws

description: Current lifecycle status of this tax line record. 'applied' = tax successfully computed and applied; 'voided' = tax line cancelled due to order void; 'adjusted' = tax amount corrected post-transaction; 'pending' = awaiting tax engine resolution; 'reversed' = tax reversed for refund.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Document Status; Oracle MICROS POS Void Processing

description: The category of tax applied to this line (e.g., sales_tax, vat, beverage_tax, bag_fee, excise_tax, service_tax). Supports differentiated tax reporting and compliance across jurisdictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_tax|vat|beverage_tax|bag_fee|excise_tax|service_tax|surcharge|environmental_fee — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: FDA Food Labeling Regulations; State Revenue Codes; IRS Publication 334

description: The portion of the order subtotal that is subject to this specific tax line, expressed in the transaction currency. Excludes non-taxable items, exempt amounts, and tax-inclusive components. Used for tax calculation validation.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IRS Publication 334; State Revenue Codes

description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental loads, and audit trail.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards; Databricks Delta Lake CDC

description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line was voided, if applicable. Populated only when tax_status = 'voided'. Used for void audit trail and financial reversal processing.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Void Processing; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Reversal

description: Unique identifier for the catering order. Primary key.
Identifier of the pre-defined catering package selected by the guest, if applicable.

Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Catering contracts define pricing and terms; linking catering orders to contracts enables contract compliance, revenue recognition, and audit reporting.
Identifier of the corporate billing account if the catering order is billed to a corporate customer.
Identifier of the guest or corporate account placing the catering order.
Identifier of the guest or corporate account placing the catering order.
Identifier of the sales representative or catering coordinator who managed this order.

Identifier of the restaurant location fulfilling this catering order.
Remaining balance due after deposit, to be paid before or at event delivery.

Scheduled start time for the catering event, used for delivery or pickup timing.

Gratuity or service charge amount included in the catering order total.

Payment instrument used for the catering order transaction.

Total tax amount applied to the catering order based on delivery location and applicable tax rates.

Unique identifier for the order channel. Primary key for the order_channel reference master.
Date when this order channel was first activated and made available for order placement across the restaurant network.
Benchmark average ticket time (order placement to fulfillment) in seconds for this channel, used for Speed of Service (SOS) performance tracking. Null if not applicable.

High-level classification of the channel by service model: dine_in (FOH counter, table service), takeout (DT, counter pickup), delivery (3PD, own delivery), catering (bulk orders), hybrid (multi-mode channels like kiosk).
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the order channel (e.g., POS, DT, OLO, 3PD, KIOSK, MOBILE, CATERING). Used as the business identifier for integration and reporting.
Detailed description of the order channel, including its purpose, typical use cases, and any special operational considerations.
Full descriptive name of the order channel (e.g., Point of Sale, Drive-Thru, Online Ordering, Third-Party Delivery, Kiosk, Mobile App, Catering).

Ownership classification of the channel: owned (company-operated channels like POS, DT, proprietary OLO), third_party (external platforms like 3PD aggregators), hybrid (co-branded or white-label solutions).
Commission or transaction fee percentage charged by the channel provider (applicable to 3PD channels). Null for owned channels. Used for COGS and P&L analysis.
Timestamp when this order channel record was first created in the system.
Date when this order channel was deactivated or retired. Null if the channel is still active.
Default daypart (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night) typically associated with this channel. Null if the channel operates across all dayparts equally.
Numeric sort order for displaying this channel in reporting dashboards and user interfaces. Lower numbers appear first.

Primary method by which orders placed through this channel are fulfilled: dine_in (guest consumes on-premise), takeout (guest picks up), delivery (delivered to guest location), curbside (pickup at designated area), catering (scheduled bulk delivery).
Name of the technology platform or system that powers this channel (e.g., Oracle MICROS, Olo, DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, proprietary mobile app). Null for native POS channels.
Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is currently active and available for order placement. False indicates the channel has been retired or temporarily disabled.
Boolean flag indicating whether this is a digital channel (true for OLO, mobile app, kiosk, 3PD; false for traditional POS, DT counter service).

Boolean flag indicating whether orders from this channel are routed to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for BOH preparation tracking. True for most channels; may be false for catering orders prepared off-site.
Boolean flag indicating whether orders from this channel must be assigned to a specific restaurant location at order time (true for delivery, catering; false for walk-in POS).
Boolean flag indicating whether this channel captures guest identification and contact information (true for OLO, mobile, 3PD; false for anonymous POS transactions).
Boolean flag indicating whether this channel supports integration with the customer loyalty program for points accrual and redemption.
reference: CRM and Loyalty Program Integration Standards

description: Boolean flag indicating whether payment is collected at the time of order placement (true for OLO, 3PD, mobile; false for traditional counter service where payment occurs at pickup).
Boolean flag indicating whether this channel allows customers to schedule orders for future fulfillment (true for catering, some OLO platforms; false for immediate-service channels like DT).

business_glossary_term: Target Speed of Service (SOS) in Seconds
description: Target Speed of Service (SOS) threshold in seconds for this channel, representing the operational goal for order fulfillment time. Used for performance benchmarking and SOP compliance.
Name of the third-party delivery service provider if this channel is a 3PD channel (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, Postmates). Null for non-3PD channels.
Timestamp when this order channel record was last modified.
Unique identifier for the order_daypart data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).
Foreign key linking to order.guest_order. Business justification: order_daypart represents the assignment of a daypart to an order; the child table should hold the FK to the parent order.
Unique identifier for the delivery order record. Primary key.
Reference to the third-party delivery (3PD) platform or first-party delivery service provider (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, proprietary fleet).
Reference to the courier or driver assigned to deliver the order. May be null if not yet assigned.
Reference to the parent order being fulfilled via delivery. Links to the core order transaction.
Reference to the restaurant location fulfilling the delivery order.
Reference to the restaurant location fulfilling the delivery order.
Actual time in minutes taken by the courier to deliver the order from pickup to guest, calculated from picked up timestamp to actual delivery timestamp.
Actual date and time when the order was delivered to the guest, as confirmed by the courier or delivery platform.
Actual time in minutes taken by the restaurant to prepare the order, calculated from order confirmation to ready for pickup.
Date and time when this delivery order record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this delivery order (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).
Free-text feedback provided by the guest about the delivery experience. Used for quality improvement and service recovery.
Guest rating for the delivery experience on a scale of 1 to 5. Used for Net Promoter Score (NPS) and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) tracking.
Primary street address line for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).
Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).
City name for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).
Three-letter ISO country code for the delivery destination (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).
Calculated distance in kilometers from the restaurant to the delivery destination. Used for delivery fee calculation and logistics analysis.

Type of exception or issue encountered during delivery fulfillment. Used for quality tracking and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|late_delivery|missing_items|wrong_address|customer_unavailable|order_damaged|cancelled_by_customer|cancelled_by_restaurant — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Delivery fee charged to the guest for the delivery service. Excludes taxes and tips.
Special instructions provided by the guest for delivery (e.g., gate code, building entrance, leave at door). May contain personally identifiable information (PII).
Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for routing optimization and distance calculation. Contains location PII.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for routing optimization and distance calculation. Contains location PII.
Postal or ZIP code for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).
State or province code for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).

Current lifecycle status of the delivery order. Tracks progression from order placement through final delivery or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|preparing|ready_for_pickup|picked_up|in_transit|delivered|cancelled|failed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Estimated time in minutes for the courier to deliver the order from pickup to guest, as provided by the delivery platform routing algorithm.
Estimated date and time for delivery to the guest, as communicated by the delivery platform or internal routing system.
Estimated time in minutes for the restaurant to prepare the order, as communicated to the delivery platform.
Free-text notes describing the delivery exception or issue. Captured by courier, restaurant staff, or customer service.
Indicates whether the delivery was requested as contactless (leave at door). True if contactless, False otherwise.
Date and time when the restaurant confirmed acceptance of the delivery order.
Date and time when the delivery order was placed by the guest via the online ordering (OLO) or third-party delivery (3PD) platform.
Date and time when the courier picked up the order from the restaurant location.
Total commission amount paid to the third-party delivery platform for this order. Business-confidential financial information.
Commission rate (as a decimal) charged by the third-party delivery platform on the order subtotal. Business-confidential pricing information.
External order identifier provided by the third-party delivery platform. Used for reconciliation and support inquiries with 3PD providers.
URL to the proof of delivery image or document (e.g., photo of order at doorstep). Used for dispute resolution.
Date and time when the order was marked as ready for courier pickup in the Kitchen Display System (KDS) or Point of Sale (POS).
Tip amount provided by the guest to the courier. May be pre-tip or post-delivery adjustment.
Total elapsed time in minutes from order placement to actual delivery. Key Speed of Service (SOS) metric for delivery orders.
Date and time when this delivery order record was last modified.
Primary key for delivery_platform
Self-referencing FK on delivery_platform (parent_delivery_platform_id)
Typical time in minutes from order acceptance to delivery completion for this platform.
Short alphanumeric code used to reference the platform in internal systems.
Date when the contractual agreement with the platform expires or is terminated (nullable for open‑ended contracts).
Date when the contractual agreement with the platform became effective.
Timestamp when the delivery platform record was first created in the system.
Standard commission fee charged by the platform as a percentage of order value.
Technical method used to exchange data with the platform.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the delivery platform record.
Official name of the delivery platform (e.g., DoorDash, UberEats).
Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks about the platform.
Date the platform was first integrated and made available for order routing.
Classification of the platform based on ownership and integration model.
Current operational status of the delivery platform.
Primary email address for technical or operational support contacts at the platform.
Primary phone number for technical or operational support contacts at the platform.
Unique identifier for each drive-thru event record. Primary key for the drive_thru_event product.
Identifier of the employee who served the customer at the time of the event (e.g., order taker, cashier, or expediter).
Identifier of the order associated with this drive-thru event, linking to the order captured in Oracle MICROS POS.
Identifier of the Oracle MICROS POS terminal that processed the order associated with this drive-thru event.
Identifier of the restaurant location where the drive-thru event occurred.
Identifier of the restaurant location where the drive-thru event occurred.
The operational business date for the drive-thru event, which may differ from the calendar date for late-night operations (e.g., 2 AM transaction may belong to the previous business day).
Total time in seconds from the vehicles initial menu board arrival to the current event. Represents the running total ticket time for the vehicle.
reference: NRA ServSafe Speed of Service Standards

description: Time-of-day segment when the drive-thru event occurred: breakfast, lunch, afternoon, dinner, or late_night. Used for daypart-specific Speed of Service (SOS) benchmarking and throughput analysis.

Time elapsed in seconds from the previous event in the drive-thru sequence for this vehicle. Used to calculate stage-specific Speed of Service (SOS) metrics such as menu board time, window time, and total ticket time.
reference: NRA ServSafe Speed of Service Standards

description: Precise date and time when the drive-thru event occurred, captured by drive-thru timer sensors integrated with Oracle MICROS POS. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) calculation.

Type of drive-thru event being recorded: menu_board_arrival (vehicle arrives at menu board), order_placed (order confirmed), pull_forward (vehicle directed to wait), window_arrival (vehicle reaches service window), payment_complete (payment transaction finalized), food_handoff (order delivered to customer), departure (vehicle exits drive-thru lane). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: menu_board_arrival|order_placed|pull_forward|window_arrival|payment_complete|food_handoff|departure — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether this drive-thru event represents an exception or anomaly (e.g., sensor malfunction, manual override, system error). True if exception occurred, False for normal operation.
Description of the reason for the exception if exception_flag is True (e.g., sensor malfunction, customer service recovery, order modification, system timeout).
Indicates whether the restaurant location is franchise-owned (True) or company-owned (False). Used for comparative Speed of Service (SOS) analysis between franchise and company-owned locations.
Ticket number assigned by the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for the order associated with this drive-thru event, used to correlate drive-thru timing with kitchen preparation timing.
Identifier for the specific drive-thru lane where the event occurred (e.g., 1 for single-lane, 1 or 2 for dual-lane configurations).

Channel through which the order was originally placed: drive_thru (ordered at menu board), mobile_order_ahead (pre-ordered via mobile app for drive-thru pickup), or third_party_delivery (3PD order picked up via drive-thru).
Number of individual items in the order associated with this drive-thru event. Used to analyze the relationship between order complexity and Speed of Service (SOS).
Total monetary value of the order associated with this drive-thru event, used for Average Check Value (ACV) calculation and revenue analysis by Speed of Service (SOS) performance.

Method of payment used for the order associated with this drive-thru event: cash, credit_card, debit_card, mobile_payment, or gift_card. Used to analyze the impact of payment method on Speed of Service (SOS).

Indicates whether the vehicle was asked to pull forward to a waiting area during this drive-thru sequence. True if pull-forward occurred, False otherwise. Pull-forwards impact Speed of Service (SOS) metrics and throughput.
Number of vehicles in the drive-thru lane at the time of this event, used to analyze the relationship between queue depth and Speed of Service (SOS) performance.
Timestamp when this drive-thru event record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.
Timestamp when this drive-thru event record was last updated in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.
Unique identifier of the physical sensor or timer device that captured this event (e.g., menu board sensor, window sensor, exit sensor).

Brand-defined target time in seconds for this specific drive-thru event type or stage (e.g., 25 seconds for menu board to order placed, 180 seconds for total ticket time). Used for performance benchmarking.
reference: NRA ServSafe Speed of Service Standards

description: Difference in seconds between the actual elapsed time and the SOS target for this event. Positive values indicate slower-than-target service; negative values indicate faster-than-target service.
reference: NRA ServSafe Speed of Service Standards

description: Name of the source system that generated this drive-thru event record (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, drive-thru timer system vendor name).
Sequential number assigned to each vehicle entering the drive-thru lane during the business day, used for tracking vehicle progression and throughput analysis.

Weather condition at the time of the drive-thru event, captured to analyze the impact of weather on Speed of Service (SOS) and throughput. Values include clear, rain, snow, fog, wind, extreme_heat, extreme_cold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|snow|fog|wind|extreme_heat|extreme_cold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Unique identifier for the Speed of Service target record.
Reference to the brand (QSR, casual, fine-dining) for which this SOS target is defined.
Reference to the restaurant location to which this SOS target applies. May be null if the target is a brand-wide standard.
Reference to the SOS target that supersedes this target. Null if this is the current active target.
Reference to the restaurant location to which this SOS target applies. May be null if the target is a brand-wide standard.
The order type to which this SOS target applies: dine_in, takeout, delivery, or all order types.
The username or employee identifier of the person who approved this SOS target for operational use.
The timestamp when this SOS target was approved for operational use.
The minimum percentage of orders that must meet this SOS target for the restaurant to be considered compliant (e.g., 85.00 means 85% of orders must meet the target).
The timestamp when this SOS target record was first created in the system.
The daypart (time segment of the day) to which this SOS target applies: breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, or all_day.
The date on which this SOS target expires or is superseded by a new target. Null indicates an open-ended target.
The date from which this SOS target becomes effective and should be used for performance evaluation.
Boolean flag indicating whether this SOS target is currently active and should be used for performance measurement and KDS compliance evaluation.
The unit of measure for SOS target times: seconds or minutes. Typically seconds for QSR and fast-casual, minutes for fine-dining.
The username or employee identifier of the person who last modified this SOS target record.
The timestamp when this SOS target record was last modified.
Free-text notes providing additional context, rationale, or special instructions for this SOS target.
Boolean flag indicating whether this SOS target applies only during peak hours (true) or throughout the entire daypart (false).
The priority level of this SOS target for operational compliance and performance evaluation: critical, high, medium, or low.
The restaurant format type to which this SOS target applies: QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual, fine_dining, fast_casual, or food_court.
The service channel to which this SOS target applies: DT (Drive-Thru), counter (Front of House counter service), OLO (Online Ordering), 3PD (Third-Party Delivery), catering, or kiosk.
Business identifier code for the SOS target, used for external reference and reporting (e.g., DT_LUNCH_QSR, COUNTER_DINNER_CASUAL).
The target time in seconds from order ready (on KDS) to order handoff to guest or delivery driver.
Human-readable name for the SOS target (e.g., Drive-Thru Lunch Target, Counter Service Dinner Target).
The target time in seconds a guest should wait in queue before placing an order, applicable to counter and drive-thru channels.
The target time in seconds from order placement to order ready on the Kitchen Display System (KDS), representing the kitchen preparation speed target.
The target total time in seconds from order placement to order fulfillment (handed to guest or delivered), representing the complete Speed of Service (SOS) target.

Classification of the SOS target: brand_standard (corporate-mandated), local_goal (restaurant-specific operational goal), franchise_goal (franchisee-defined target), or regulatory (mandated by health or safety regulations).
The version number of this SOS target, incremented each time the target is revised or updated.
The percentage threshold below which a warning alert is triggered for operational management (e.g., 80.00 triggers a warning if compliance falls below 80%).
Boolean flag indicating whether this SOS target should be adjusted based on adverse weather conditions that impact throughput.
The username or employee identifier of the person who created this SOS target record.
Unique identifier for the operating hours record. Primary key.
Identifier of the user who approved this operating hours schedule. Used for audit trail and compliance tracking.
Identifier of the user who approved this operating hours schedule. Used for audit trail and compliance tracking.
Identifier of the labor scheduling template associated with these operating hours. Links to workforce scheduling system for Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) planning and shift assignment.
Identifier of the restaurant unit to which these operating hours apply. Links to the restaurant master record.
Identifier of the restaurant unit to which these operating hours apply. Links to the restaurant master record.
The date and time when this operating hours schedule was approved, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail and compliance tracking.
The time the restaurant closes for the specified day and daypart, in 24-hour HH:mm format (e.g., 22:00). May extend past midnight for late-night operations.

Detailed sub-classification within the finding category. Examples: temperature_control, hand_washing, equipment_calibration, uniform_compliance, waste_tracking. Provides granular segmentation for trend analysis.

reference: FDA Food Code, OSHA Standards, Local Health Department Regulations

Numeric priority ranking used when a member qualifies for multiple segments and campaign assignment logic requires a single segment selection. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used in offer conflict resolution.
