description: Business reason for account closure. Examples: member request, fraud, inactivity, duplicate account, policy violation, data privacy request.
Timestamp when the loyalty member record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.
Timestamp when the loyalty member record was last modified. Audit field for data lineage and change tracking.
Total number of achievement badges earned by the member through gamification activities, challenges, and milestones.
Month of the member's birthday (1-12). Stored without year or day for privacy. Used for birthday reward campaigns and personalized offers.
Current available points balance that the member can redeem. Calculated as lifetime earned minus lifetime redeemed minus expired points.
Current membership tier level in the loyalty program hierarchy. Determines benefits, rewards multipliers, and exclusive offers available to the member.
Date when the member provided explicit consent for data collection, processing, and storage under applicable privacy regulations.
Indicates whether the member has consented to receive physical direct mail offers and communications. True if opted in, False if opted out.
Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional emails. True if opted in, False if opted out.
Channel through which the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. OLO (Online Ordering), POS (Point of Sale), mobile app, website, in-restaurant kiosk, or call center.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS

description: Date when the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the start of the member lifecycle.
Current level in the loyalty program gamification system. Members progress through levels by completing challenges, earning badges, and accumulating points.
Date of the most recent loyalty program activity by the member, including points earned, points redeemed, or profile updates. Used for churn prediction and reactivation campaigns.
Date of the most recent purchase transaction where the member earned or redeemed points. Subset of last_activity_date focused on revenue-generating activity.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Total cumulative loyalty points earned by the member since enrollment. Never decreases; used for tier qualification and lifetime value analysis.
Total cumulative loyalty points redeemed by the member for rewards, discounts, or offers since enrollment.
Externally-facing unique membership identifier displayed on loyalty cards, mobile app, and customer communications. Human-readable alphanumeric code.
Date when the next batch of points will expire. Null if no points are scheduled to expire.
Most recent Net Promoter Score provided by the member, ranging from 0 (detractor) to 10 (promoter). Measures member satisfaction and likelihood to recommend.
Date when the most recent NPS survey was completed by the member.
Number of points scheduled to expire within the next 30 days. Used for proactive member engagement and retention campaigns.
Member's preferred language for communications and app interface. Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code.

Current lifecycle status of the loyalty membership. Active: member in good standing. Suspended: temporarily blocked due to policy violation. Churned: member opted out or account closed. Inactive: no activity for extended period. Pending: enrollment initiated but not completed.
Indicates whether the member has consented to receive push notifications through the mobile app. True if opted in, False if opted out.
Unique referral code assigned to this member for member-get-member campaigns. When shared and used by new enrollees, both parties receive bonus points.
Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional SMS text messages. True if opted in, False if opted out.
Business reason or explanation for the current program status. Examples: fraud detection, member request, inactivity, policy violation, system migration.
Date when the member accepted the loyalty program terms and conditions. Required for legal compliance and dispute resolution.
Version number of the loyalty program terms and conditions that the member accepted. Format: v1.0, v2.1, etc.
Indicates whether the member has consented to sharing their data with third-party partners for marketing purposes. True if opted in, False if opted out.
Date when the member achieved or was assigned their current tier level. Used for tier anniversary calculations and renewal cycles.
Date when the current tier status expires and member will be re-evaluated for tier qualification. Null for lifetime tiers.
Total number of restaurant visits or orders placed by the member since enrollment. Includes dine-in, drive-thru, OLO, and 3PD orders where loyalty was applied.
Unique identifier for the referral event. Primary key.
Identifier for the marketing campaign or promotional period under which this referral was created. Supports campaign-level ROI analysis.
Restaurant location where the referred guest completed enrollment. Null for online enrollments. Supports location-level referral performance tracking.
Guest who received the referral invitation. May be pre-enrollment or post-enrollment guest identifier.
Loyalty member ID assigned to the referred guest upon successful enrollment conversion. Null if referral has not yet converted.
Loyalty member who initiated the referral. Links to the member who shared the referral code or invitation.
Guest who received the referral invitation. May be pre-enrollment or post-enrollment guest identifier.
Restaurant location where the referred guest completed enrollment. Null for online enrollments. Supports location-level referral performance tracking.
Channel through which the referral invitation was shared. Supports attribution of viral growth mechanics across digital and physical touchpoints.
Date when the referred guest successfully enrolled in the loyalty program and the referral converted. Null if referral has not yet converted.
Precise timestamp when the referred guest completed enrollment and the referral status changed to converted. Null if not yet converted.
Timestamp when this referral record was first created in the loyalty system. Audit trail for record creation.
Date when the referral code expires and can no longer be used for enrollment. Supports time-bound referral campaigns and urgency mechanics.
Total amount of the referred member first purchase. Supports Average Check Value (ACV) lift analysis for referred members.
Date of the referred member first purchase transaction after enrollment. Used to measure referral quality and time-to-first-purchase.
Indicates whether this referral was flagged as fraudulent by automated or manual review. True if fraudulent activity detected.
Description of the fraud detection reason if fraud_flag is true. Examples include duplicate device, self-referral, velocity abuse, or manual review findings.
Timestamp when this referral record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
Optional personalized message included by the referring member when sharing the referral. Supports engagement analysis and sentiment tracking.
User ID or system identifier that last modified this referral record. Supports audit trail and accountability.
Unique alphanumeric code used by the referring member to track the referral. Generated by the loyalty platform and shared via referral channels.
Date when the referring member initiated the referral event. Represents the business event timestamp for referral creation.

Current lifecycle status of the referral. Pending indicates awaiting conversion; converted indicates successful enrollment; expired indicates referral window closed; fraudulent indicates detected abuse; cancelled indicates referrer or system cancellation.
Precise timestamp when the referral was created in the loyalty system. Supports detailed event sequencing and time-of-day analysis.
Date when the referred member bonus points were credited to the new member account. Null if bonus not yet awarded.
Loyalty points awarded to the referred member upon successful enrollment. Incentivizes new member acquisition and first-transaction engagement.
Date when the referrer bonus points were credited to the referring member account. Null if bonus not yet awarded.
Loyalty points awarded to the referring member upon successful referral conversion. Supports referral program ROI measurement and member incentive tracking.
Digital platform or system where the referral was initiated. Supports omnichannel referral attribution and platform performance analysis.
Version of the referral program terms and conditions accepted by the referring member at the time of referral creation. Supports compliance and audit trails.
Unique identifier for the loyalty membership tier. Primary key.
Annual membership fee charged to maintain this tier status, if applicable. Null or zero indicates no fee. Used for premium tier monetization.

Comma-separated or structured text summary of key benefits and perks associated with this tier (e.g., free delivery, birthday rewards, exclusive offers, priority support). Used for member communications and benefit entitlement logic.
Indicates whether members in this tier receive a special birthday reward (e.g., free item, bonus points, discount). True if eligible, false otherwise.
Hexadecimal color code used for tier branding in digital channels (e.g., #CD7F32 for bronze, #C0C0C0 for silver, #FFD700 for gold). Used in mobile app, website, and marketing materials.
Timestamp when this tier record was first created in the loyalty system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Minimum threshold value required to maintain this tier status during re-evaluation period. Falling below this value triggers downgrade to lower tier. Null indicates no downgrade policy.
Indicates whether members in this tier receive early access to new Limited Time Offers (LTO) before general availability. True if eligible, false otherwise.
Date when this tier configuration was retired or replaced. Null indicates the tier is currently active. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.
Date when this tier configuration became active and available for member assignment. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.
Indicates whether members in this tier receive access to exclusive promotional offers and Limited Time Offers (LTO) not available to lower tiers. True if eligible, false otherwise.
Indicates whether members in this tier are eligible for free delivery on Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD) orders. True if eligible, false otherwise.
Number of days after tier expiration during which members retain tier benefits before downgrade. Used to provide buffer period for re-qualification.
URL path to the tier badge or icon image asset used in member portal, mobile app, and digital communications. Supports tier visual identity and gamification.
Timestamp when this tier record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.
Date when this tier was officially launched to the market and communicated to guests. May differ from effective_start_date for soft launches or pilot programs.
Maximum percentage discount that can be applied when redeeming points for rewards in this tier (e.g., 20.00 for 20% max discount). Used to control reward economics and prevent abuse.
Username or identifier of the user who last modified this tier configuration. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Multiplier applied to base points accrual for members in this tier (e.g., 1.0 for base, 1.5 for 50% bonus, 2.0 for double points). Used to calculate accelerated points earning.
Indicates whether members in this tier receive priority customer support (e.g., dedicated phone line, faster response times). True if eligible, false otherwise.
The metric used to determine tier qualification (points accrued, visit count, total spend amount, or transaction count). Defines how qualification_threshold is interpreted.
Number of days over which the qualification threshold must be met (e.g., 365 for annual qualification, 90 for quarterly). Null indicates lifetime accumulation.
Numeric threshold value required to qualify for this tier (e.g., 1000 points, 25 visits, $500 spend). Interpretation depends on qualification_metric.
Bonus points awarded to members in this tier for each successful referral of a new loyalty program member. Used to incentivize member acquisition through word-of-mouth.
Indicates whether unused points can roll over to the next qualification period for members in this tier. True if rollover is allowed, false if points expire at period end.

Integer defining the hierarchical display order of tiers (e.g., 1 for Bronze, 2 for Silver, 3 for Gold, 4 for Platinum). Lower values represent entry-level tiers; higher values represent premium tiers.

Description of the target guest segment this tier is designed to attract and retain (e.g., frequent visitors, high spenders, digital-first guests, family diners). Used for marketing strategy and program design.
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM). Used as business identifier in operational systems and reporting.
Detailed description of the tier, including value proposition, benefits summary, and marketing messaging. Used in loyalty program communications and member portal.
Human-readable display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Bronze Member, Silver Member, Gold Member, Platinum Elite). Used in guest-facing communications and marketing materials.

Current lifecycle status of the tier. Active tiers are available for member assignment; inactive tiers are temporarily disabled; retired tiers are no longer offered but may have legacy members; pending tiers are configured but not yet launched.
Indicates whether members should receive automated notification when they qualify for upgrade to this tier. True if notification is enabled, false otherwise.
Number of days a member retains this tier status after qualification before re-evaluation (e.g., 365 for annual renewal). Null indicates permanent tier assignment.
One-time bonus points awarded to a member upon first achieving this tier. Used to incentivize tier progression and enhance member engagement.
Unique identifier for each tier change event in the loyalty program. Primary key for the tier history transactional audit log.
Reference to the specific order, purchase, or points transaction that triggered this tier change event. Null for manual adjustments or scheduled reviews.
Reference to the loyalty program member who experienced this tier change event.
Reference to the marketing campaign that granted this promotional tier upgrade. Null for non-promotional tier changes.
Reference to the restaurant location where the triggering transaction occurred. Null for tier changes not associated with a specific location (e.g., scheduled reviews, manual adjustments).
Reference to the restaurant location where the triggering transaction occurred. Null for tier changes not associated with a specific location (e.g., scheduled reviews, manual adjustments).
The date and time when this tier history record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit tracking.
The date when the new tier status became active and the member began receiving benefits associated with the new tier level.
The date when this tier status is scheduled to expire, requiring re-qualification or resulting in tier review. Null indicates indefinite or lifetime tier status.

Indicates whether this tier change was manually processed by an administrator or customer service representative rather than automatically triggered by system rules. True for manual adjustments, false for automated changes.
Indicates whether this tier change was the result of a promotional campaign or marketing offer rather than organic qualification. True for promotional upgrades, false for earned tier changes.
The date and time when this tier history record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
Indicates whether benefits associated with the new tier were immediately activated and available for member use. Used for benefit provisioning tracking.
The loyalty tier code the member transitioned to as a result of this change event (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).
The communication channel used to notify the member of this tier change: email, SMS, push notification, in-app message, physical mail, or none if no notification was sent.
Indicates whether a tier change notification was successfully sent to the member via email, mobile app push, or other communication channel. Used for member engagement tracking.
The date and time when the tier change notification was sent to the member. Used for communication audit and member experience analytics.
The name or employee identifier of the administrator who authorized a manual tier override. Required for audit compliance when is_manual_override is true.
Business justification and approval notes for manual tier overrides, required for regulatory audit trails and program fairness documentation.
Indicates whether benefits associated with the previous tier were immediately revoked upon tier change. Used for benefit lifecycle management and member experience tracking.
The loyalty tier code the member held immediately before this change event (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).
The channel through which this tier change was processed: POS (Point of Sale), OLO (Online Ordering), mobile app, web portal, call center, or admin portal. Used for channel analytics.
The ending date of the measurement period used to evaluate tier qualification criteria.
The beginning date of the measurement period used to evaluate tier qualification criteria (e.g., rolling 12 months, calendar year).
The total loyalty points balance at the time of tier change that contributed to qualification or demotion decision.
The cumulative spend amount that qualified the member for this tier change, measured in the program's base currency. Used for tier progression analytics.
The number of restaurant visits or transactions during the qualification period that contributed to this tier change.
The operational system that originated this tier change event (e.g., Olo Digital Ordering Platform, Oracle MICROS POS, Salesforce CRM). Used for data lineage and system integration tracking.
The unique identifier of this tier change record in the source operational system, used for data reconciliation and troubleshooting.
Additional free-text notes or comments about this tier change event, used for customer service context and audit documentation.
Business-readable identifier for this tier change event, used for customer service reference and audit tracking.

Standardized code indicating the business reason for the tier change: qualification (earned through spend/visits), demotion (failed to maintain requirements), manual_override (customer service adjustment), promotional_upgrade (marketing campaign), anniversary_renewal (annual tier review), points_threshold (points-based qualification), spend_threshold (spend-based qualification), visit_threshold (frequency-based qualification).
Detailed human-readable explanation of why this tier change occurred, providing context for customer service and audit purposes.
The precise date and time when the tier change event was processed and recorded in the loyalty system.

Classification of the tier movement direction and nature: upgrade (promotion to higher tier), downgrade (demotion to lower tier), lateral (same tier level renewal), initial_enrollment (first tier assignment), reinstatement (reactivation after lapse), manual_adjustment (administrative override).
The number of days this tier status is valid, calculated as the difference between effective date and expiry date. Used for tier lifecycle analytics.
Business-readable reference number of the transaction that triggered this tier change, used for customer service inquiries and audit trails.
Unique identifier for the loyalty program enrollment event. Primary key for the enrollment event transaction.
Reference to the staff member who assisted with the enrollment. Null for self-service enrollments. Used for staff performance tracking.
Reference to the marketing campaign that drove the enrollment. Null if enrollment was not campaign-driven. Enables campaign ROI analysis.
Reference to the restaurant unit where the enrollment occurred. Null for digital-only enrollments without a specific unit association.
Reference to the staff member who assisted with the enrollment. Null for self-service enrollments. Used for staff performance tracking.
Original transaction identifier from the source system. Used for reconciliation and traceability back to the source system.
Original transaction identifier from the source system. Used for reconciliation and traceability back to the source system.
Reference to the membership tier assigned at enrollment. Typically the base tier for new members, but may be elevated tier for transfer or upgrade enrollments.
Reference to the loyalty program member who enrolled. Links to the member master record.
Reference to the loyalty program the member enrolled in. Supports multi-program environments.
Reference to the restaurant unit where the enrollment occurred. Null for digital-only enrollments without a specific unit association.
Reference to the specific welcome offer or promotional reward issued at enrollment. Null if no offer was issued.
Date and time when this enrollment event record was first created in the data platform. System audit field for data lineage.
Indicates whether the member opted in to receive email communications at enrollment. Required for CAN-SPAM compliance.

Channel through which the enrollment occurred. OLO (Online Ordering) for web/app orders, POS (Point of Sale) for in-restaurant, mobile_app for native app, kiosk for self-service terminals, staff_assisted for employee-facilitated, web for website.

Country where the enrollment occurred. ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country codes. Used for geographic segmentation and compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|AUS|JPN|CHN|BRA — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Type of device used to complete the enrollment. Supports channel and user experience analysis.
Geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude) of the enrollment location if captured. Format: latitude,longitude. Used for location-based analytics.
IP address from which the enrollment was initiated. Used for fraud detection and geographic analysis. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.

Language preference selected by the member at enrollment. ISO 639-2 three-letter language codes. Used for personalized communications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: eng|spa|fra|deu|ita|por|zho|jpn|kor — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Free-text notes or comments about the enrollment event. Used for special circumstances, exceptions, or customer service annotations.
Business-facing unique enrollment transaction number. Format: ENR followed by 10 digits. Used for customer service and audit tracking.
Name of the source system that captured the enrollment event. Examples: Olo, Oracle MICROS, Salesforce CRM, Mobile App. Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.

Current lifecycle status of the enrollment event. Completed indicates successful enrollment; pending indicates awaiting verification; failed indicates enrollment did not complete; cancelled indicates member-initiated cancellation; reversed indicates system or compliance reversal.
Date and time when the enrollment event occurred in the source system. Represents the real-world business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps.
Type of enrollment action. New indicates first-time enrollment; re_enrollment indicates returning member; transfer indicates program migration; upgrade indicates tier promotion enrollment.

Status of fraud detection checks performed at enrollment. Passed indicates clean enrollment; failed indicates suspected fraud; pending indicates checks in progress; not_required indicates no checks were performed.
Numeric fraud risk score assigned to the enrollment event by fraud detection systems. Range typically 0-100, with higher scores indicating higher risk. Null if fraud checking was not performed.
Number of loyalty points awarded to the member at enrollment as a sign-up bonus. Zero if no points were awarded.
Indicates whether the member opted in to receive marketing communications at enrollment. True if opted in; False if declined.
Indicates whether the member opted in to receive mobile app push notifications at enrollment.
Specific referral or campaign code used during enrollment. Enables attribution tracking for member-get-member programs and promotional campaigns.

Source that drove the enrollment. Organic for unprompted; referral_code for member referral; campaign for marketing campaign; social_media for social channels; email for email marketing; in_store for restaurant promotion; partner for third-party partner. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic|referral_code|campaign|social_media|email|in_store|partner — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether the member opted in to receive SMS/text message communications at enrollment. Required for TCPA compliance.
Indicates whether the member accepted the loyalty program terms and conditions at enrollment. Required for compliance and legal purposes.
Version identifier of the terms and conditions accepted by the member at enrollment. Enables tracking of which terms version the member agreed to.
Date and time when this enrollment event record was last modified in the data platform. System audit field for change tracking.
Indicates whether required verification has been completed. True if completed; False if pending or not completed.
Date and time when enrollment verification was completed. Null if verification is not required or not yet completed.
Indicates whether the enrollment requires additional verification (e.g., email verification, identity verification). True if verification is required; False if not required.
Indicates whether a welcome offer or sign-up bonus was issued to the member upon enrollment. True if issued; False if not issued.
Unique identifier for the loyalty program. Primary key.
Number of bonus points awarded to members on their birthday. Zero if no birthday bonus is offered.
Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the program is available (e.g., USA,CAN,MEX). Null for global programs.
Timestamp when the program record was first created in the system.
Name of the loyalty currency used in the program (e.g., Stars, Points, Coins, Miles, Rewards).
Monetary value of each loyalty point when redeemed. Used to calculate redemption value and liability.
Date when the loyalty program was discontinued or sunset. Null for active programs.
Number of bonus points awarded to new members upon successful enrollment. Zero if no enrollment bonus is offered.
Comma-separated list of channels through which guests can enroll in the program (e.g., mobile_app, website, pos, kiosk, third_party).
Indicates whether the program includes gamification elements such as challenges, badges, streaks, or missions.
Geographic coverage of the loyalty program: global (all markets), regional (multi-country region), country (single country), state (sub-national), or local (specific metro/franchise group).
Date when the loyalty program was officially launched and became available for guest enrollment.
Email address of the program manager for internal contact and escalation purposes.
Name of the business owner or manager responsible for the loyalty program strategy and performance.
Minimum number of points required to make a redemption. Used to prevent micro-redemptions and manage transaction costs.
Timestamp when the program record was last modified in the system.
Indicates whether the program is integrated with online ordering platforms for digital points accrual and redemption.
Ownership structure of the loyalty program: corporate (company-owned only), franchise (franchise-owned only), or hybrid (both corporate and franchise participation).
Indicates whether the program uses guest data to deliver personalized offers, recommendations, and communications.
Number of months after which unused loyalty points expire. Null if points never expire.
Number of loyalty points earned per dollar spent. Used to calculate points accrual from transaction amounts.

business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Integration Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether the program is integrated with POS systems for in-store points accrual and redemption.
Web URL to the privacy policy explaining how member data is collected, used, and protected.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the loyalty program used in integrations and reporting (e.g., REWARDS_PLUS, STAR_CLUB).
Detailed description of the loyalty program value proposition, benefits, and how it works. Used for internal reference and guest communications.
Marketing name of the loyalty program displayed to guests (e.g., Rewards Plus, Star Club, VIP Circle).

Current operational status of the loyalty program: active (accepting enrollments and transactions), inactive (not operational), suspended (temporarily paused), pilot (testing phase), or sunset (being phased out).

Classification of the loyalty program structure: points-based (earn and redeem points), visit-based (frequency rewards), hybrid (combination), subscription (paid membership), or tiered (status levels).
Number of bonus points awarded to members who successfully refer a new member. Zero if no referral program exists.
Comma-separated list of restaurant formats where the program is valid (e.g., QSR, casual_dining, fine_dining, food_truck, ghost_kitchen).
Monthly or annual subscription fee required for paid membership programs. Null for free programs.
Billing frequency for subscription-based programs: monthly, annual, or one-time. Null for free programs.
Description of the primary guest segment the program is designed to attract and retain (e.g., frequent diners, families, business travelers, value seekers).
Web URL to the official terms and conditions document governing the loyalty program.
Indicates whether the program supports points accrual and redemption through third-party delivery platforms.
Indicates whether the program includes membership tiers with progressive benefits (True) or is a single-tier program (False).
Primary key for the ProgramCampaignAllocation association
Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign
Foreign key linking to the loyalty program
Budget amount allocated to the program for this campaign
Guest segment targeted by the campaign within the program
Unique identifier for the loyalty points accrual rule. Primary key.
Marketing campaign identifier if this accrual rule is part of a promotional campaign. Null for evergreen base earning rules. Used for campaign performance tracking and ROI analysis.
Foreign key linking to loyalty.program. Business justification: Accrual rules belong to a specific loyalty program; linking accrual_rule to program enables program‑level governance of rules.
User ID of the loyalty program manager or business owner who approved this rule for activation. Null for draft rules. Used for governance and approval workflow tracking.
Date and time when this accrual rule was approved for activation. Null for draft or unapproved rules. Part of governance audit trail.

Order channel(s) where this accrual rule is active. All = applies across all channels, POS = Point of Sale (Point of Sale) in-store, OLO = Online Ordering (Online Ordering) web/app, Mobile_app = native mobile application, Kiosk = self-service kiosk, Drive_thru = drive-through lane, 3PD = Third-Party Delivery (Third-Party Delivery) platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|pos|olo|mobile_app|kiosk|drive_thru|3pd — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date and time when this accrual rule record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail.
Comma-separated list of daypart codes (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night) to which this rule applies. Empty or null = applies to all dayparts. Used for time-of-day promotional earning.

The calculation basis for points accrual. Dollar_spent = points per currency unit, Transaction_count = points per visit, Item_count = points per menu item purchased, Fixed_event = flat points for qualifying event.
Date when this accrual rule expires and stops awarding points. Null = no expiration (evergreen rule). Part of the rule's validity period.
Date when this accrual rule becomes active and begins awarding points. Part of the rule's validity period.
Comma-separated list of menu item codes, categories, or member segments explicitly excluded from this rule. Used to carve out exceptions (e.g., alcohol, gift cards, discounted items).
Flat number of points awarded for qualifying events (e.g., 500 points for birthday, 100 points for survey completion). Used when earning_basis is fixed_event. Null for variable earning rules.
Comma-separated list of franchisee IDs when franchise_scope is specific_franchisee. Null for all other franchise scopes.

Ownership model scope for this rule. All = applies to all restaurants, Company_owned = corporate locations only, Franchise = franchisee locations only, Specific_franchisee = limited to designated franchisee IDs (stored in franchise_id_list).
Comma-separated list of country codes, region codes, or restaurant location codes where this rule is active. Empty or null = applies globally. Used for market-specific promotions.
Cap on the number of points a member can earn per calendar day under this rule. Null = no daily cap. Used for frequency-based earning controls.
Lifetime or campaign-period cap on points a single member can earn under this rule. Null = no member cap. Used for limited-time offers and promotional campaigns.
Cap on the number of points that can be earned in a single transaction under this rule. Null = no cap. Used to prevent abuse and manage liability.
Comma-separated list of loyalty tier codes (e.g., bronze, silver, gold, platinum) eligible for this rule. Empty or null = applies to all tiers. Used for tier-exclusive earning opportunities.

Comma-separated list of menu category codes to which this rule applies. Empty or null = applies to all categories. Used to restrict earning to specific product categories (e.g., beverages, entrees, LTO items).
Comma-separated list of specific menu item codes to which this rule applies. Empty or null = applies to all items within category scope. Used for item-specific promotions.
Minimum transaction subtotal required to qualify for this accrual rule. Null = no minimum threshold. Used for spend-tier promotions.
User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this accrual rule. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Date and time when this accrual rule record was last updated. Part of audit trail for change tracking.
Free-text field for additional business context, implementation notes, or special instructions related to this accrual rule. Used by loyalty program managers for operational documentation.
Number of days from earning date after which points awarded by this rule expire. Null = points do not expire. Used for promotional points with limited validity.
Number of loyalty points awarded per unit of the earning basis (e.g., 10 points per dollar spent, 50 points per transaction). Null for fixed_event earning basis.
Indicates whether members must explicitly opt in or activate this rule to earn points. True = member action required, False = automatically applied to eligible transactions.
Business-readable unique code for the accrual rule (e.g., BASE_EARN, TIER_BONUS, BIRTHDAY_PROMO). Used for operational reference and system integration.
Detailed description of the accrual rule, including business logic, conditions, and intended use case.
Human-readable name of the accrual rule for display and reporting purposes.

Numeric priority for conflict resolution when multiple accrual rules apply to the same transaction. Lower number = higher priority. Used to determine which rule takes precedence (e.g., promotional rule overrides base earning).

Current lifecycle state of the accrual rule. Draft = under development, Active = currently awarding points, Paused = temporarily disabled, Expired = past effective_end_date, Archived = retired and no longer in use.

Category of earning trigger that activates this accrual rule. Purchase = transaction-based earning, Visit = frequency-based earning, Referral = member-get-member, Birthday = anniversary reward, Survey = feedback incentive, Signup = enrollment bonus.
Indicates whether this rule can be combined with other accrual rules in the same transaction. True = can stack with other rules, False = mutually exclusive (highest priority rule wins).
Indicates whether member tier multipliers (e.g., Gold = 1.5x, Platinum = 2x) apply to this accrual rule. True = tier bonus applies, False = base earning only.
Version number of this accrual rule for change tracking and auditability. Incremented each time the rule is modified. Supports rule versioning and rollback.
User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this accrual rule. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Unique identifier for the loyalty program challenge. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Connects challenges to the franchisee that creates them, enabling franchise‑level challenge participation tracking and budgeting.
Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Challenges run at particular restaurant locations; linking supports site‑level challenge tracking and ROI analysis.
Username or identifier of the manager or director who approved this challenge for activation. Required for governance and compliance.
Date and time when this challenge was approved for launch. Null if still in draft status awaiting approval.

Total marketing budget allocated for this challenge in dollars, including reward costs and promotional expenses. Used for Return on Investment (ROI) and Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) impact analysis.
Internal system code for the challenge used for tracking and integration across Point of Sale (POS) and Online Ordering (OLO) channels.
Detailed description of the challenge rules, objectives, and how members can participate. Displayed in loyalty app and Online Ordering (OLO) platform.
Marketing name of the gamification challenge displayed to loyalty members (e.g., 'Weekend Warrior', '5-Day Streak', 'Menu Explorer').

The unique transaction identifier from the originating source system. Enables end-to-end traceability and reconciliation between the loyalty ledger and upstream systems. Format varies by source system.

Identifier of the processing batch or job that created this ledger entry. Used for bulk operations such as nightly expiration runs, mass bonus awards, or system migrations. Null for real-time individual transactions.
The fiscal period (month or accounting period number) within the fiscal year when this transaction occurred. Supports monthly financial close, P&L reporting, and loyalty liability accrual tracking.

The fiscal year in which this points transaction occurred, derived from transaction_timestamp. Supports financial reporting, GAAP revenue recognition for breakage, and year-over-year loyalty program analytics.

Flag indicating whether this transaction has been voided or reversed by a subsequent transaction. True if a reversal exists, false otherwise. Immutable ledger entries are never deleted, only marked as voided.

The total monetary value of the order that triggered this points transaction, in the local currency. Used to calculate points earn rate and for financial reconciliation. Null for non-order transactions.

The cumulative running balance of points for this member immediately after this transaction was applied. Provides audit trail and reconciliation capability. Must equal prior balance plus points_delta.

The signed integer change in points balance for this transaction. Positive values represent accruals (earn, bonus), negative values represent deductions (redeem, expire, adjust downward). Zero-delta entries are not permitted.

The rate at which points were earned per currency unit spent. Example: 1.0000 means 1 point per dollar, 2.0000 means 2 points per dollar (double points promotion). Populated for earn transactions, null otherwise.

The date on which the points earned in this transaction will expire if not redeemed. Populated only for earn and bonus transaction types. Null for redeem, expire, and adjust transactions. Supports FIFO expiration logic and member communication.

The date and time when this ledger entry was committed to the loyalty system of record. May differ from transaction_timestamp for batch processing or delayed reconciliation. Used for ETL audit and SLA monitoring.
Human-readable store or unit number for the restaurant location. Denormalized for operational reporting and guest service. Null for non-location-specific transactions.

Date when this reward becomes available for redemption by loyalty members. Used for Limited Time Offer (LTO) rewards and seasonal promotions.
Indicates whether this reward can be combined with other promotional offers, coupons, or discounts in a single transaction. True if combinable, False if exclusive.

Direct cost to the business to fulfill this reward, including food cost, packaging, and direct labor. Used for profitability analysis, loyalty program Return on Investment (ROI) calculation, and financial accrual. Expressed in USD.
Timestamp when this reward record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Specifies which daypart(s) this reward can be redeemed during: breakfast, lunch, dinner, late night, or all day. Used to manage product mix (PMIX) and drive traffic during specific service periods.

Type of discount mechanism applied: percentage (e.g., 10% off), fixed amount (e.g., $5 off), free item (no charge), buy-one-get-one (BOGO), or none (for non-discount rewards like merchandise or experiences).

Numeric value of the discount. For percentage discounts, this is the percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). For fixed amount discounts, this is the dollar amount (e.g., 5.00 for $5 off). Null for free items and non-discount rewards.

Indicates whether this reward is featured prominently in the loyalty app and marketing campaigns. True if featured, False if standard catalog placement. Used to drive awareness and redemption of strategic rewards.

Comma-separated list of restaurant format types where this reward is valid (e.g., QSR, casual, fine-dining, food court). Null if applicable to all formats. Used when restaurant_applicability_scope is 'specific_formats'.
URL path to the reward image asset displayed in the loyalty app, Online Ordering (OLO) platform, and marketing materials. Used for visual merchandising and guest engagement.
Comma-separated list of market codes or geographic regions where this reward is valid. Null if applicable to all markets. Used when restaurant_applicability_scope is 'specific_markets'.
Minimum transaction amount required to redeem this reward. Null if no minimum purchase is required. Used to protect Average Check Value (ACV) and prevent low-value transactions.
User identifier or system account that last modified this reward record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when this reward record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.
Estimated dollar value equivalent of the reward for financial reporting, liability accrual, and Average Check Value (ACV) impact analysis. Expressed in USD.
Name of the third-party partner providing this reward (for partner offers). Null for internally-fulfilled rewards. Used for co-branded rewards and strategic partnerships.
External offer code or voucher code provided by the partner for redemption tracking. Null for internally-fulfilled rewards.
Number of loyalty points required to redeem this reward. Used to calculate point liability and drive guest engagement.
Maximum number of times a single loyalty member can redeem this reward during the availability window. Null indicates no limit.
Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed exclusively through the branded mobile app (distinct from general OLO). True if eligible, False if not.
Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed at Drive-Thru (DT) service points. True if eligible, False if not.
Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed through Online Ordering (OLO) platforms including web and mobile app. True if eligible, False if not.

business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Redemption Channel Eligibility
description: Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed at Point of Sale (POS) terminals in restaurant locations. True if eligible, False if not.
Indicates whether this reward can be redeemed through Third-Party Delivery (3PD) platforms such as DoorDash, Uber Eats, or Grubhub. True if eligible, False if not.
Total number of times this reward has been redeemed by loyalty members since inception. Used for popularity analysis, inventory planning, and loyalty program performance reporting.

Defines which restaurant units can honor this reward: all units (system-wide), specific markets (geographic regions), specific formats (Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual, fine-dining), franchise only, or company-owned only.
Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the reward, used in Point of Sale (POS) and Online Ordering (OLO) systems for redemption lookup.
Detailed description of the reward, including terms, conditions, and guest-facing messaging used in marketing materials and the loyalty app.
Human-readable name of the reward displayed to guests in the loyalty app, Online Ordering (OLO) platform, and Point of Sale (POS) system.

Current lifecycle status of the reward: active (available for redemption), inactive (temporarily unavailable), pending (scheduled for future activation), expired (past availability end date), or discontinued (permanently removed from catalog).

Classification of the reward by category: food item (free menu item), beverage (free drink), discount (percentage or dollar off), merchandise (branded goods), experience (event access, VIP treatment), or partner offer (third-party rewards).

Indicates how sales tax is applied to this reward: taxable (tax calculated on reward value), non-taxable (no tax applied), or tax included (tax already embedded in points cost). Required for accurate Point of Sale (POS) processing and financial reporting.
Legal terms and conditions governing the redemption and use of this reward. Includes restrictions, exclusions, and compliance language required by Federal Trade Commission (FTC) regulations.
Specifies which loyalty membership tier(s) are eligible to redeem this reward. Used to create tier-exclusive rewards that drive tier progression and engagement.
Maximum total number of redemptions allowed across all loyalty members for this reward. Used for limited-supply rewards and exclusive experiences. Null indicates no limit.
User identifier or system account that created this reward record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Primary key for the combo_component association
Foreign key linking to the combo_meal
Foreign key linking to the menu_item
Indicates if the item is highlighted in the combo presentation
Adjusted price for the menu_item when sold as part of the combo
Number of units of the menu_item included in the combo
Order in which the item appears within the combo listing
Unique surrogate identifier for the combo meal record. Primary key for the combo_meal data product in the menu domain.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Combo Promotion: associate combo meals with marketing campaigns that promote them, supporting campaign budgeting, scheduling, and sales attribution.
Foreign key linking to menu.menu. Business justification: Combo meals are defined within a specific menu; adding menu_id ties the combo to its parent menu.

Comma-separated list of major allergens present in the combo meal based on its default component items (e.g., 'milk,wheat,soy'). Supports FDA major food allergen labeling requirements and guest advisory communications. Detailed per-allergen declarations are managed in the allergen_declaration product.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); ISO 22000

description: Name or employee identifier of the menu management authority who approved this combo meal for publication. Supports menu governance, audit trail, and compliance with internal SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) for menu changes.
reference: Internal Menu Governance SOP; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Date on which the combo meal was formally approved for publication by the designated menu authority. Supports audit trail and menu governance compliance.

The monetary discount provided to the guest by purchasing the combo meal versus buying each component item individually (individual_items_price_sum minus bundle_price). Key metric for menu engineering and promotional value analysis.
reference: GAAP Revenue Recognition; FTC Advertising Regulations

description: The retail selling price of the combo meal as a bundle, charged to the guest at POS or digital ordering channels. This is the price the guest pays for the full combo, distinct from the sum of individual item prices.
reference: GAAP Revenue Recognition; Oracle MICROS POS Pricing

description: Maximum calorie count for the combo meal when customizable components are at their highest-calorie configuration. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance when combos offer substitution options.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101)

description: Minimum calorie count for the combo meal when customizable components are at their lowest-calorie configuration. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance when combos offer substitution options.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101)

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the combo meal, used in Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, and 3PD integrations for menu lookup and order capture. Serves as the canonical cross-system reference code.

Detailed description of the combo meal contents and value proposition, used for digital menu boards, Olo OLO platform, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) channel listings. Supports guest decision-making and marketing copy.

Human-readable marketing name of the combo meal as displayed on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering channels (e.g., 'Classic Value Meal', 'Family Pack', 'Kids Meal'). Used for guest-facing display and reporting.
Current lifecycle status of the combo meal offering. Controls whether the combo is available for sale across channels. 'test' indicates a market-test combo not yet in full rollout.

Classification of the combo meal by bundle format. Drives menu engineering analysis, PMIX reporting, and promotional targeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: value_meal|family_pack|kids_meal|party_pack|snack_combo|premium_combo — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this combo meal is offered. Supports global menu management, regulatory compliance, and multi-currency pricing.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; GAAP/IFRS International Reporting

description: Timestamp when the combo meal record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage in the Silver layer.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary fields on this combo meal record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global franchise markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting

description: Daypart(s) during which this combo meal is available for sale. Daypart segmentation is a core foodservice operational concept used in PMIX analysis, labor scheduling, and revenue management.
The date on which the combo meal was or is scheduled to be removed from the menu. Nullable for permanent menu fixtures. Used for menu lifecycle management and supply chain wind-down planning.
The date on which the current version of the combo meal record ceases to be operationally effective. Nullable for open-ended records. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.
reference: Internal Menu Governance Policy; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: The date from which the current version of the combo meal record (including pricing, components, and availability) is operationally effective. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.
reference: Internal Menu Governance Policy; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Cost of Goods Sold as a percentage of the bundle price (item_cost / bundle_price * 100). Core menu engineering KPI used to classify combo meals and drive pricing decisions. Aligns with CoGS% jargon in foodservice P&L management.
reference: GAAP COGS Accounting; NRA Financial Performance Standards

description: URL of the primary product image for the combo meal used on digital menu boards, Olo OLO platform, and 3PD channel listings. Must be a publicly accessible HTTPS URL.

The sum of the base prices of all individual component menu items if purchased separately. Used to calculate the bundle discount amount and communicate value to guests. Sourced from Oracle MICROS POS item pricing.
reference: GAAP Revenue Recognition; FTC Advertising Regulations

description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available through Third-Party Delivery (3PD) partners (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub). Controls 3PD channel menu syndication via Olo integration.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Channel Management Policy

description: Indicates whether guests can substitute or modify component items within the combo meal (e.g., swap side item, upsize drink). Drives modifier group display logic in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo.
Indicates whether this combo meal is available for dine-in service. Relevant for casual and fine-dining formats where combo availability may differ by service mode.
Indicates whether this combo meal is available for sale through the Drive-Thru (DT) channel. Controls channel-specific menu display in Oracle MICROS POS and KDS routing.
Indicates whether this combo meal is part of a national rollout versus a regional or test-market offering. Drives marketing campaign scope, supply chain planning, and franchise communication.
reference: Internal NRO (New Restaurant Opening) and Launch Policy

description: Indicates whether this combo meal is available through the Olo Digital Ordering Platform (OLO) for web and mobile ordering. Controls digital menu availability.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Channel Management Policy

description: Indicates whether the combo meal bundle price is subject to sales tax. Drives tax calculation logic in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA AR module. Tax applicability may vary by jurisdiction.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Tax Compliance; SAP S/4HANA AR Module

description: Total Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the combo meal, representing the combined food cost of all component items. Used to calculate COGS% and support P&L and menu engineering decisions. Sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management and SAP S/4HANA CO module.
reference: GAAP COGS Accounting; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: The date on which the combo meal became or is scheduled to become available for sale. Used for menu lifecycle management, supply chain lead time planning, and marketing campaign alignment.

Menu engineering quadrant classification for the combo meal based on profitability and popularity: 'star' (high profit, high popularity), 'plow_horse' (low profit, high popularity), 'puzzle' (high profit, low popularity), 'dog' (low profit, low popularity). Core menu engineering decision-support field.
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; Kasavana & Smith Menu Engineering Model

description: Olo Digital Ordering Platform external identifier for this combo meal. Used for menu synchronization between the internal menu master and the Olo platform for web, mobile, and 3PD channel ordering.
Indicates whether this combo meal is available at franchise-operated locations, company-owned locations, or both. Supports franchise compliance tracking via FranConnect and royalty reporting.
reference: FranConnect Franchise Management; IFA Franchise Best Practices

description: Target Product Mix (PMIX) percentage representing the expected share of total transactions this combo meal should represent within its category. Used for menu performance benchmarking and sales forecasting.
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; Internal PMIX Reporting

description: Oracle MICROS POS-specific item code used to ring up the combo meal at the point of sale. This is the PLU/button code used by FOH (Front of House) staff and KDS (Kitchen Display System) routing.
Geographic region code where this combo meal is available (e.g., 'NORTHEAST', 'WEST', 'SOUTH'). Supports regional menu variation, pricing strategy, and PMIX analysis across the restaurant network.

Restaurant format(s) for which this combo meal is applicable. QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) combos differ structurally from casual or fine-dining bundles. Drives menu engineering and franchise compliance.
Display sequence order for the combo meal on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering interfaces. Lower values appear first. Controls guest-facing menu presentation.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this combo meal record originated (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance and Lineage Policy

description: Tax category code assigned to the combo meal for sales tax calculation in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA. Determines applicable tax rates by jurisdiction and product type.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Tax Compliance; SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration

description: Total calorie count for the combo meal as configured with default component items. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance on menus with 20+ locations. Displayed on menu boards and digital ordering platforms.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); NRA ServSafe

description: Timestamp when the combo meal record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver layer.
Unique surrogate identifier for each published menu record in the lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Menu versioning is driven by a menu manager; capturing the creator enables change‑control and reporting.
Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee‑specific menu versions are tracked for regional offerings; the Franchisee Menu Management process needs this FK.
Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: OPERATIONS: The entire menu is governed by a HACCP plan that drives safety checks across all items.
Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Required for site‑specific menu publishing, compliance, and pricing; each restaurant location (site) maintains its own active menu.
Foreign key linking to franchise.territory. Business justification: Regional Menu Planning uses territory assignment to determine which menu set applies to each geographic area.
Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Required for POS and reporting to assign each menu to the specific restaurant unit where it is served.

Indicates whether this menu requires allergen declarations for the nine FDA-recognized major food allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame). Supports HACCP compliance and guest safety.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); HACCP

description: Current workflow state of the menu in the approval lifecycle. Menus must be approved before publishing. Tracks progression from draft through review, approval, and eventual archival.
reference: Internal Menu Governance Policy; FDA Food Labeling Regulations 21 CFR Part 101

description: Name or employee identifier of the individual who granted final approval for this menu version. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance with FDA food labeling and franchise disclosure obligations.
reference: FDA Food Labeling Regulations 21 CFR Part 101; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations

description: Date and time when the menu received final approval, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Establishes the official approval event in the menu lifecycle audit trail.
reference: FDA Food Labeling Regulations 21 CFR Part 101; Internal Audit Policy

description: Total number of menu categories (e.g., Burgers, Sides, Beverages, Desserts) included in this menu version. Supports menu structure analysis and POS display configuration.

The ordering or service channel this menu is published for. Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), dine-in, kiosk, and catering channels may carry different item availability, pricing, and promotional content. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dine_in|drive_thru|olo|third_party_delivery|kiosk|catering|curbside|mobile_app — promote to reference product]
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the country where this menu is published. Drives regulatory compliance (FDA, USDA, local health departments), currency, and language localization.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; FDA Food Labeling Regulations; USDA

description: Date and time when this menu record was first created in the system, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Establishes the beginning of the menu's audit trail.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all pricing on this menu (e.g., 'USD', 'CAD', 'GBP'). Ensures consistent financial reporting and P&L (Profit and Loss) attribution across markets.

The meal period or daypart this menu is active for. Daypart segmentation drives kitchen prep, staffing, and PMIX (Product Mix) analytics. Standard dayparts include breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, snack, and all-day.
The local wall-clock time (HH:MM, 24-hour) at which this menu's daypart ends service. Used by POS and KDS to automatically deactivate the menu and transition to the next daypart.
The local wall-clock time (HH:MM, 24-hour) at which this menu's daypart begins service. Used by POS and KDS (Kitchen Display System) to automatically switch active menus.

Indicates whether this menu is configured for display on digital menu boards in restaurant locations. Digital menu boards require specific image assets, layout templates, and real-time price synchronization.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management; Internal Operations SOP

description: The calendar date on which this menu version is retired and no longer available for ordering. Null indicates an open-ended menu with no planned expiry. Critical for LTO (Limited Time Offer) lifecycle management.
reference: Internal Menu Governance Policy; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations

description: The calendar date on which this menu version becomes active and available for ordering. Governs menu rollout scheduling for New Restaurant Openings (NRO), Limited Time Offers (LTO), and seasonal rotations.
reference: Internal Menu Governance Policy; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations

description: Menu engineering classification based on the Boston Consulting Group (BCG) matrix adapted for foodservice: Stars (high popularity, high margin), Plow Horses (high popularity, low margin), Puzzles (low popularity, high margin), Dogs (low popularity, low margin). Drives strategic menu optimization decisions.
reference: NRA (National Restaurant Association) Menu Engineering Standards

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Reviewed Indicator
description: Indicates whether this menu version has undergone a HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) review to ensure all items meet food safety critical control point requirements before publication.
reference: HACCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 120; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management Systems

description: Indicates whether this menu is the default active menu for its restaurant format, channel, and daypart combination. Only one menu per format-channel-daypart combination should be flagged as default at any point in time.

Indicates whether this menu is designated for franchise-operated restaurants as opposed to company-owned locations. Franchise menus may have restricted customization rights governed by the Franchise Disclosure Document (FDD).
reference: FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations; IFA (International Franchise Association) Best Practices

description: Indicates whether this menu is a Limited Time Offer (LTO) menu with a defined promotional window. LTO menus drive incremental traffic and require distinct PMIX tracking, marketing support, and supply chain coordination.
reference: NRA (National Restaurant Association) Menu Engineering Standards

description: Total number of distinct menu items included in this menu version. Used in menu engineering analysis to assess menu complexity, PMIX (Product Mix) breadth, and operational throughput impact.
reference: NRA (National Restaurant Association) Menu Engineering Standards

description: Identifier for the KDS (Kitchen Display System) routing profile associated with this menu. Determines how orders are routed to BOH (Back of House) preparation stations based on item categories and prep sequences.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Internal Kitchen Operations SOP

description: IETF BCP 47 language code for the menu's display language (e.g., 'en-US', 'fr-CA', 'es-MX'). Supports multilingual menu publishing for diverse guest populations and regulatory bilingual requirements.
reference: IETF BCP 47; FDA Food Labeling Regulations 21 CFR Part 101

description: Date and time when this menu record was most recently updated, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: The geographic market or region this menu is scoped to (e.g., 'US-Southeast', 'Canada', 'EMEA-UK'). Supports regional menu variation, localization, and regulatory compliance across jurisdictions.
reference: Internal Geographic Hierarchy; FDA 21 CFR; USDA Regional Compliance

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the menu, used in Oracle MICROS POS configurations, franchise communications, and cross-system references (e.g., BRKFST-QSR-DT-2024).
Free-text narrative description of the menu's purpose, scope, and key characteristics. Used in internal documentation, franchise communications, and menu engineering review sessions.
Human-readable display name of the menu as it appears in POS systems, digital ordering platforms, and internal reporting (e.g., 'Breakfast Drive-Thru Menu Q1 2024').

Free-text field for operational notes, change justifications, or franchise-specific instructions associated with this menu version. Used by menu managers and franchise business consultants during review cycles.

Indicates whether this menu requires calorie and nutritional information disclosure per FDA menu labeling regulations (21 CFR Part 101.11). Mandatory for restaurant chains with 20 or more locations in the United States.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Final Rule 21 CFR Part 101.11; ACA Section 4205

description: The native menu identifier as configured in the Olo Digital Ordering Platform. Enables reconciliation of digital channel menu versions with the master menu record.

Indicates whether Product Mix (PMIX) sales tracking is enabled for this menu version. When enabled, POS transaction data is aggregated to measure item-level sales velocity, supporting menu engineering and COGS% analysis.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Internal Revenue Management Policy

description: The native menu identifier as configured in Oracle MICROS POS. Used for system-of-record reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the POS operational system.

Pricing tier classification for this menu, reflecting the overall price positioning strategy (value, standard, premium, luxury). Drives ACV (Average Check Value) benchmarking and revenue management decisions.
reference: Internal Revenue Management Policy; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Current publishing state of the menu indicating whether it is live and visible to guests across channels. Distinct from approval_status — a menu may be approved but not yet published, or suspended post-publication.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Date and time when the menu was pushed live to POS systems, digital ordering platforms (OLO), and third-party delivery (3PD) integrations. Critical for SSS (Same-Store Sales) period alignment and rollout governance.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: The restaurant service format this menu is designed for. Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR) menus differ structurally from casual or fine-dining menus in item count, pricing tiers, and daypart coverage.
reference: NRA (National Restaurant Association) Restaurant Classification Standards

description: Date and time when the menu was deactivated and retired from all channels. Null if the menu is still active. Used for historical PMIX analysis and LTO post-campaign reporting.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this menu record originated (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, FranConnect). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Sequential integer version of the menu, incremented each time the menu is revised and republished. Enables rollback, audit, and comparison of menu changes over time.
Unique surrogate identifier for each menu item record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the menu_item master data product.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Item Master

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Campaign Planning: associate specific menu items with marketing campaigns (e.g., seasonal featured dishes) to enable menu rollout, budgeting, and performance reporting.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Menu item creation workflow requires recording the employee (chef/manager) who created the item for audit and traceability.
Foreign key linking to foodsafety.allergen_profile. Business justification: COMPLIANCE: Each menu item must map to an allergen control profile for labeling and guest safety.

Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee Menu Customization Report requires linking each menu item to the owning franchisee for localized availability and pricing decisions.

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue reporting requires each menu item to map to a GL account for posting sales; finance teams use this for daily sales ledger reconciliation.
Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: REGULATORY: HACCP compliance per menu item is required for safety reporting and audit traceability.

Foreign key linking to inventory.item_category. Business justification: Enables category‑level procurement and menu‑planning reports by aligning menu item categories with inventory item_category hierarchy, a standard practice in restaurant supply chain management.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Required for POS and inventory forecasting: each menu item must be linked to its underlying stock item (SKU) to reconcile sales with inventory levels and generate daily sales‑vs‑stock reports.

Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Supplier sourcing for each menu item is needed for cost analysis, procurement planning, and supplier performance dashboards; experts expect a direct supplier_id on menu_item.

Pipe-delimited list of the FDA's 9 major food allergens present in this menu item (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree_nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame). Required for FDA allergen labeling compliance and guest safety disclosures on OLO and 3PD platforms. Example: 'wheat|milk|soy'.

description: The standard retail selling price of the menu item before any discounts, promotions, or channel-specific pricing adjustments. Expressed in the local operating currency. This is the principal quantitative value (MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE) for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

description: Total caloric content of the menu item in kilocalories (kcal) per standard serving. Mandatory disclosure on menus for restaurant chains with 20+ locations under FDA Menu Labeling Rule (Section 4205 of the ACA).

description: Standard cost of goods sold (COGS) for this menu item, representing the total ingredient and packaging cost per serving. Used to calculate CoGS% and gross margin in P&L reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA CO and MarketMan BOM costing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Standard Cost; MarketMan BOM Costing; GAAP/IFRS

description: Timestamp when this menu item record was first created in the system of record (Oracle MICROS POS). Represents the business creation event, not the ETL load time. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the base_price and cost fields (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across international franchise markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting Standards

description: The daypart(s) during which this menu item is available for sale. Daypart segmentation drives kitchen prep scheduling, labor planning, and PMIX reporting by time-of-day. Standard foodservice dayparts: breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, snack, all_day.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration; NRA Daypart Standards

description: The date this menu item was permanently discontinued and removed from the menu. Null for active items. Used for menu lifecycle management, historical PMIX analysis, and supply chain wind-down planning.

description: URL of the approved product image for this menu item used on digital menus, OLO platform, 3PD marketplace listings, and marketing materials. Must reference the approved brand asset in the digital asset management system.

description: Indicates whether this menu item is available through Third-Party Delivery (3PD) partners (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub). Controls item syndication to 3PD marketplace menus via Olo integration.

description: Indicates whether this menu item can be included as a component in a combo meal offering. Used by menu engineering teams to configure combo meal bundles and upsell prompts in POS and OLO.

description: Indicates whether this menu item supports guest customization (e.g., add/remove ingredients, size selection, preparation modifications). Drives modifier group configuration in Oracle MICROS POS and OLO ordering flows.
