Date when the tag assignment expires or is removed
Date when the tag assignment becomes effective for the menu item
Indicates whether this tag is the primary dietary classification for the menu item
Unique surrogate identifier for each menu engineering review event. Primary key for this transactional record.
Identifier of the senior leader or menu director who formally approved the review findings and recommended actions. Nullable until review reaches approved status.
Identifier of the employee or analyst who conducted and owns this menu engineering review. References the workforce record in Workday HCM. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction.
reference: Workday HCM; NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices

description: Identifier of the primary menu category (e.g., Burgers, Beverages, Desserts) that is the focal scope of this review. Aligns with Oracle MICROS POS menu category structure.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices

description: Identifier of the employee or analyst who conducted and owns this menu engineering review. References the workforce record in Workday HCM. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction.
reference: Workday HCM; NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices

description: Indicates whether any recommended changes require an allergen declaration review prior to implementation. Ensures compliance with FDA allergen labeling requirements and protects guest safety.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); ISO 22000

description: Average contribution margin (selling price minus Cost of Goods Sold) across all items evaluated in this review, expressed in the local currency. Used as the profitability threshold for engineering classification. Classified confidential as it reflects internal pricing strategy.
reference: GAAP/IFRS; NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices

description: Average popularity index (typically based on PMIX share relative to expected mix) across all items evaluated in this review. Used as the demand threshold for engineering classification into Stars, Plowhorses, Puzzles, or Dogs.

Sales channel(s) in scope for this review: Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), dine-in, or all channels. Impacts pricing and availability decisions made during the review.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%) Threshold
description: The Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%) threshold applied during this review to classify items as high or low cost. Items exceeding this threshold may be flagged for repricing or reformulation. Classified confidential as it reflects internal cost management strategy.
reference: GAAP/IFRS; NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices

description: Timestamp when this menu engineering review record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data governance and lineage tracking in the Silver layer.
Daypart(s) in scope for this review: breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, or all dayparts. Daypart-level PMIX and contribution margin data drives engineering decisions for each time window.
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices; Oracle MICROS POS

description: The menu engineering analytical framework applied during this review. Common frameworks include the BCG matrix, Kasavana-Smith (Stars/Plowhorses/Puzzles/Dogs), or a custom proprietary model. Determines how items are classified by popularity and profitability.
Indicates whether any items evaluated in this review require a food safety or HACCP review prior to implementation of changes. Triggers a Zenput audit task when true.
reference: FDA Food Safety Regulations; HACCP; ISO 22000; Zenput

description: Current status of the implementation of menu changes recommended by this review. Tracks whether actions are pending, in progress, completed, or deferred to a future cycle.
Target date by which the recommended menu changes from this review are expected to be implemented across in-scope restaurants and channels. Drives project planning and POS update scheduling.
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices; Oracle MICROS POS

business_glossary_term: Is Comparable Store Sales (SSS) Impact Assessed Flag
description: Indicates whether a Same-Store Sales (SSS) impact assessment was performed as part of this review. Ensures that menu changes are evaluated for their effect on comparable store sales performance before implementation.
reference: GAAP/IFRS; NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices

description: Indicates whether the review outcomes and recommended actions apply to franchise-operated restaurants in addition to company-owned locations. Drives franchise communication and compliance workflows in FranConnect.
reference: FranConnect; IFA Franchise Best Practices; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations

description: Number of menu items recommended for discontinuation during this review. Drives menu simplification and SKU rationalization initiatives.
Total number of menu items formally evaluated during this review session. Provides scope sizing for the review and supports review efficiency tracking.
Number of menu items assigned a 'keep' engineering decision during this review. Contributes to menu complexity and portfolio stability metrics.
Number of menu items recommended for repositioning (e.g., menu placement, daypart shift, channel availability change) during this review.
Number of menu items recommended for repricing during this review. Feeds into the pricing update workflow in Oracle MICROS POS.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices

description: Timestamp when this menu engineering review record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer, and audit compliance.

business_glossary_term: Limited Time Offer (LTO) Items Evaluated Count
description: Number of Limited Time Offer (LTO) items specifically evaluated during this review. Supports LTO pipeline governance and performance assessment.
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices; Oracle MICROS POS

description: Outcome decision for the LTO pipeline resulting from this review: advance to launch, hold for further development, cancel, extend the offer window, or convert to a core menu item.
Projected or actual menu complexity score for the in-scope category or group after implementation of review recommendations. Used to quantify the operational simplification impact of the review.

Quantitative menu complexity score (e.g., total active SKU count or a weighted complexity index) for the in-scope category or group prior to this review. Enables before/after complexity reduction measurement.
Scheduled date for the next menu engineering review for the same scope (category/channel/format). Supports the ongoing menu governance calendar and review cycle management.
Indicates whether any recommended changes (e.g., reformulation, new items) require a nutritional analysis update before implementation. Ensures compliance with FDA menu labeling regulations.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Regulations (21 CFR Part 101); NRA ServSafe

description: Source system from which Product Mix (PMIX) sales data was extracted for this review. Ensures traceability of the analytical inputs used in engineering decisions.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Internal notes documenting the pricing strategy rationale applied during this review, including competitive positioning, value perception, and margin optimization considerations. Classified confidential as it contains proprietary pricing strategy.
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices; GAAP/IFRS

description: Free-text narrative summarizing the key recommended actions arising from this review, including pricing changes, item removals, repositioning strategies, and LTO decisions. Intended for executive reporting and menu governance documentation.
Restaurant format applicability for this review: Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual dining, fine-dining, or all formats. Governs which restaurant types are affected by the review outcomes.
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices; IFA Franchise Best Practices

description: Identifier of the restaurant group, market cluster, or organizational unit in scope for this review. Enables multi-unit and franchise-level menu engineering analysis.
reference: FranConnect; NRA Menu Engineering Best Practices

description: Frequency cadence of this menu engineering review: monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, or ad-hoc. Supports scheduling and governance of the menu lifecycle process.
The principal business event date on which the formal menu engineering review was conducted. Distinct from record creation timestamp. Used for period-over-period analysis and review cycle tracking.

Externally-known business identifier for this review event, formatted as MER-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used for cross-referencing in communications, reports, and downstream systems such as Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA.
End date of the sales and performance data window analyzed during this menu engineering review. Together with review_period_start_date, defines the full analysis window.
Start date of the sales and performance data window analyzed during this menu engineering review. Defines the lookback period for PMIX, ACV, and contribution margin analysis.

Defines the primary dimension along which this review is scoped. Determines whether the review focuses on a menu category, a daypart (e.g., breakfast, lunch), a channel (DT, OLO, 3PD), a restaurant format (QSR, casual, fine-dining), or a geographic market.
Current lifecycle state of the menu engineering review. Tracks progression from initial drafting through approval and closure. Drives workflow routing and downstream menu update actions.
Full name of the employee or analyst who conducted this menu engineering review. Denormalized for reporting convenience and audit trail clarity.
Operational system of record from which this review record originated or was primarily driven. Supports data lineage and Silver layer traceability in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; SAP S/4HANA; FranConnect

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each 86 event record — when a menu item is temporarily pulled from service at a restaurant. Primary key of this table.

Reference to the specific kitchen equipment unit that failed and caused the 86 event, when reason_code = 'equipment_failure'. Supports Repairs and Maintenance (R&M) tracking and equipment reliability analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Fixed Assets (FA); Oracle MICROS POS — Kitchen Equipment

description: Reference to the specific kitchen equipment unit that failed and caused the 86 event, when reason_code = 'equipment_failure'. Supports Repairs and Maintenance (R&M) tracking and equipment reliability analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Fixed Assets (FA); Oracle MICROS POS — Kitchen Equipment

description: Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient_lot. Business justification: REQUIRED: Food‑safety incident reports must link an event to the specific ingredient lot used, enabling traceability and regulatory recall reporting.
Reference to the employee (Back of House (BOH) or Front of House (FOH) staff member) who first reported or logged the 86 event. Links to workforce employee master.
reference: Workday HCM — Workforce Management; OSHA Incident Reporting Standards

description: Reference to the menu item that was 86'd (removed from service). Links to the menu.menu_item master record.

Reference to the primary supplier of the ingredient or item involved in the 86 event, particularly relevant for out_of_stock and supplier_delay reason codes. Supports supply chain root cause analysis.
reference: Coupa Procurement — Supplier Management; MarketMan Inventory Management — Vendor Management

description: Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.
Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.

The service channel(s) impacted by the 86 event. 'all' = item unavailable across all channels; 'dine_in' = in-restaurant only; 'drive_thru' (DT) = drive-through only; 'olo' = Online Ordering (OLO) platform; '3pd' = Third-Party Delivery (3PD) partners such as DoorDash or Uber Eats.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS — Channel Management

description: Date and time when this 86 event record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Standards; PCI DSS Record Integrity

description: The daypart during which the 86 event was active or first occurred. Enables PMIX (Product Mix) and daypart-level impact analysis. Aligns with menu.daypart definitions.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration; NRA Daypart Standards

description: Total elapsed time in minutes from start_timestamp to end_timestamp. Populated upon resolution. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) impact analysis and operational KPI reporting.
Date and time when the menu item was restored to service and the 86 event was closed. Null if the event is still open. Used to calculate total downtime duration.
The internal team to which the 86 event was escalated, if applicable. Supports accountability tracking and cross-functional response coordination.
reference: Zenput — Operational Compliance; FranConnect — Franchise Compliance

description: Date and time when the 86 event was escalated to operations management, supply chain, or franchise support teams. Null if the event was resolved without escalation. Supports SLA and response time analysis.
reference: Zenput — Operational Compliance; FranConnect — Franchise Compliance

description: Estimated number of guest covers (transactions) that could not be fulfilled due to the 86 event during the affected period. Derived operationally at the restaurant level for guest experience impact reporting. Not a calculated KPI — captured as a manager estimate at time of resolution.
reference: NRA — Guest Experience Standards; Oracle MICROS POS — Transaction Reporting

description: Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for the 86 event (e.g., 86-2024-07-15-A3F9K2). Used in operational communications, Zenput audit tasks, and guest experience escalations.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management; Zenput — Operational Compliance

description: Current lifecycle state of the 86 event. 'open' = item currently unavailable; 'resolved' = item restored to service; 'escalated' = issue elevated to operations or supply chain team; 'cancelled' = event logged in error.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management; NRA SOP Standards

description: Indicates whether a guest-facing notification was sent (e.g., via OLO or 3PD platform) to inform customers that the item is temporarily unavailable. True = notification dispatched. Supports Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) and Net Promoter Score (NPS) impact tracking.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Guest Notifications; NRA Guest Experience Standards

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Reference
description: Reference code to the specific HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) that was breached or triggered this 86 event, if applicable. Used for food safety audit trails and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: HACCP — Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; ISO 22000

business_glossary_term: Inventory Quantity On Hand at 86 Event
description: The recorded inventory quantity on hand for the primary ingredient or item at the time the 86 event was triggered. Captured from MarketMan at event time. Supports PAR Level (Periodic Automatic Replenishment Level) analysis and stockout root cause investigation.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Stock Tracking; PAR Level Standards

description: Unit of measure for the inventory_quantity_on_hand value (e.g., each, oz, lb, kg, liter, gallon, portion). Ensures correct interpretation of the quantity field. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|oz|lb|kg|liter|gallon|portion — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Stock Tracking; USDA Measurement Standards

description: Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a food safety concern (e.g., HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) failure, quality issue, or regulatory recall). True = food safety event requiring Zenput audit task and potential regulatory notification.
reference: HACCP — Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Indicates whether the 86'd item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO). True = LTO item. 86 events on LTO items carry heightened business impact due to marketing commitments and guest expectations, and may require immediate escalation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management; NRA LTO Best Practices

description: Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a formal supplier or regulatory product recall. True = item must not be served and recall protocol must be followed per FDA/USDA guidelines.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; USDA Meat and Poultry Inspection; ISO 22000

business_glossary_term: Kitchen Display System (KDS) Alert Sent Flag
description: Indicates whether a Kitchen Display System (KDS) alert was sent to BOH staff notifying them of the 86 event. True = KDS alert dispatched. Supports BOH operational awareness and throughput management.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Kitchen Display System (KDS)

description: The item identifier in the Olo Digital Ordering Platform at the time of the 86 event. Used to confirm whether the item was also suppressed on the OLO channel during the event.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Menu Management

description: Indicates whether the item was suppressed on the Olo Digital Ordering Platform during the 86 event. True = item hidden from online ordering menu. Prevents guest orders for unavailable items on digital channels.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Menu Management

description: Indicates whether the restaurant where the 86 event occurred is a franchise location or a company-owned location. Drives escalation routing and compliance accountability.
reference: FranConnect — Franchise Compliance; IFA Franchise Best Practices

description: The Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) Level quantity set for the item at this restaurant at the time of the 86 event. Compared against inventory_quantity_on_hand to assess whether the stockout was preventable.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — PAR Level Management

description: The Point of Sale (POS) item code as configured in Oracle MICROS POS at the time of the 86 event. Captured at event time to preserve the POS state even if the menu item code changes later.

business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Item Suppressed Flag
description: Indicates whether the item was actively suppressed (hidden/disabled) on the Oracle MICROS POS system during the 86 event. True = item removed from POS ordering screens. Ensures operational accuracy and prevents staff from ringing up unavailable items.

Standardized reason code explaining why the menu item was removed from service. 'out_of_stock' = inventory depleted; 'equipment_failure' = kitchen equipment down; 'quality_issue' = item failed quality/HACCP check; 'recall' = supplier or regulatory recall; 'supplier_delay' = ingredient not delivered; 'prep_time_exceeded' = throughput constraint. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: out_of_stock|equipment_failure|quality_issue|recall|supplier_delay|prep_time_exceeded — promote to reference product]
reference: HACCP — Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; NRA SOP Standards

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context beyond the reason_code. For example, specifying which piece of equipment failed, which ingredient was out of stock, or the nature of the quality issue. Supports root cause analysis.
reference: Zenput — Operational Compliance; NRA SOP Standards

description: Job role of the employee who reported the 86 event (e.g., manager, Back of House (BOH) staff, Front of House (FOH) staff, shift lead, franchisee). Supports accountability and training gap analysis.
reference: Workday HCM — Job Role Classification; NRA ServSafe Certification

description: The corrective action taken to resolve the 86 event and restore the item to service. 'restocked' = inventory replenished; 'equipment_repaired' = kitchen equipment fixed; 'item_substituted' = alternative item offered; 'recalled_item_removed' = item permanently pulled per recall; 'supplier_order_placed' = emergency procurement initiated; 'no_action' = event closed without resolution (e.g., end of daypart). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: restocked|equipment_repaired|item_substituted|recalled_item_removed|supplier_order_placed|no_action — promote to reference product]
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management; Zenput — Operational Compliance; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act

description: The restaurant format (Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual, or fine-dining) of the location where the 86 event occurred. Supports format-level operational benchmarking.
reference: FranConnect — Franchise Development; NRA Restaurant Classification Standards

description: The operational system of record from which this 86 event was originated or captured. 'micros_pos' = Oracle MICROS POS; 'zenput' = Zenput operational compliance; 'marketman' = MarketMan inventory alert; 'olo' = Olo platform suppression event; 'manual' = manually entered by operations staff.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Zenput; MarketMan Inventory Management; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Date and time when the menu item was first removed from service (86'd). This is the principal real-world event timestamp. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.

Date and time when this 86 event record was last modified in the data platform, such as when the event was resolved, escalated, or corrected. Supports data lineage and change tracking in the Silver layer.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Standards; PCI DSS Record Integrity

description: Reference to the Zenput food safety audit task or operational compliance task created in response to this 86 event. Enables traceability between the 86 event and the corrective action workflow in Zenput.
reference: Zenput — Food Safety Audits and Operational Compliance

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each nutrition profile record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the nutrition_profile data product.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Nutrition data is compiled by a nutritionist or chef; linking to the employee enables traceability for audits.
Reference to the menu item for which this nutrition profile applies. Links the nutritional data to the specific sellable item on the menu.
Reference to the standardized recipe or Bill of Materials (BOM) version from which this nutrition profile was derived. Ensures traceability between formulation and declared nutritional values.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101 – Food Labeling; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Added sugars content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel per 2016 labeling rule update. Critical for compliance with FDA dietary guidelines and sugar-reduction reformulation tracking.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(6)(iii) – Added Sugars Declaration; FDA 2016 Nutrition Facts Rule

description: Current lifecycle state of the nutrition profile in the regulatory review and approval workflow. Only profiles in 'approved' status may be published to customer-facing menu boards and digital ordering channels (OLO, 3PD) as required by FDA menu labeling rules.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.11 – Restaurant Menu Labeling; ACA Section 4205

description: Name or employee identifier of the Registered Dietitian, Quality Assurance Manager, or Regulatory Affairs Officer who approved this nutrition profile for publication. Supports regulatory audit trail and accountability.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.11; NRA ServSafe Standards

description: Date on which the nutrition profile received final regulatory or internal quality assurance approval for publication on customer-facing menu boards and digital ordering channels. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance audit trails.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.11 – Restaurant Menu Labeling; ACA Section 4205

description: Calcium content per declared serving size in milligrams. Mandatory micronutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel per 2016 labeling rule update. Supports bone-health menu claims and dietary compliance reporting.

Upper bound of the calorie range for menu items with variability in preparation. Used alongside calorie_range_low to display a calorie range on FDA-compliant menu boards when a single calorie value is not applicable.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.11(b)(2)(ii) – Calorie Range Declaration

description: Lower bound of the calorie range for menu items with variability in preparation (e.g., customizable items, combo meals with choice components). Required for FDA menu board labeling when a single calorie value cannot be declared due to preparation variability.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.11(b)(2)(ii) – Calorie Range Declaration

description: Total caloric content per declared serving size, expressed in kilocalories (kcal). Mandatory disclosure on FDA Nutrition Facts panel and on customer-facing menu boards for restaurant chains with 20 or more locations.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(1) – Calorie Declaration; ACA Section 4205

description: Calories derived from total fat content per serving, expressed in kilocalories. Historically required on FDA Nutrition Facts panels; retained for legacy reporting, menu engineering analysis, and international market compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(1)(i) – Calories from Fat

description: Cholesterol content per declared serving size in milligrams. Mandatory nutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Critical for heart-health dietary compliance and menu engineering decisions.

Timestamp when this nutrition profile record was first created in the system. Supports regulatory audit trail, data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse, and compliance with FDA record-keeping requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management; GAAP Record Retention

description: Reference caloric intake used to calculate percent Daily Value (%DV) figures on the FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Standard FDA reference is 2,000 kcal/day for adults. Stored to support accurate %DV recalculation if FDA reference values are updated.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(d) – Percent Daily Value

description: Method by which the nutritional values in this profile were determined. Drives confidence level and regulatory defensibility of declared values. Lab analysis is the gold standard for FDA compliance; recipe calculation is acceptable for composite items. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lab_analysis|database_lookup|recipe_calculation|supplier_provided|literature_value — promote to reference product if values expand]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9 – Nutrition Labeling; USDA FoodData Central

description: Dietary fiber content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory sub-component of total carbohydrate on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Supports high-fiber menu claims and dietary guideline alignment.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(6)(i) – Dietary Fiber Declaration

description: Date from which this nutrition profile version is the active, published record for the associated menu item. Used to manage version transitions when recipes are reformulated or ingredients change, ensuring correct values are displayed on menu boards.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.11 – Restaurant Menu Labeling

description: Date on which this nutrition profile version expires and must be superseded by a new analysis or re-approval. Nullable for profiles with no defined expiry. Triggers re-analysis workflow when recipe changes occur or when regulatory re-certification is required.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Insoluble fiber content per declared serving size in grams. Voluntary sub-component of dietary fiber on FDA Nutrition Facts panel; supports digestive health menu claims.

Iron content per declared serving size in milligrams. Mandatory micronutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel per 2016 labeling rule update. Key metric for dietary adequacy reporting and menu engineering.

Indicates whether this nutrition profile record is the currently active and published version for the associated menu item. Supports Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) Type 2 versioning in the Silver Layer lakehouse, ensuring only one active profile per menu item at any point in time.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.11 – Restaurant Menu Labeling

description: Accreditation number of the third-party laboratory that performed the nutritional analysis. Supports regulatory audit defensibility and ensures analysis was conducted by an ISO 17025-accredited or FDA-recognized laboratory.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9; ISO/IEC 17025 – Laboratory Competence

description: Date on which the laboratory analysis was conducted to determine the nutritional values in this profile. Required for regulatory audit trails and to determine when re-analysis is needed due to recipe changes or ingredient substitutions.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9 – Nutrition Labeling; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Timestamp when this nutrition profile record was most recently updated. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer, and regulatory audit trail for nutrition data modifications.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Formatted calorie string as it must appear on FDA-compliant customer-facing menu boards and digital ordering platforms (e.g., '540 Cal', '430-780 Cal'). Derived from calories or calorie range fields; stored as the authoritative display string to ensure consistent rendering across POS, OLO, and 3PD channels.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.11 – Restaurant Menu Labeling; ACA Section 4205

description: Monounsaturated fat content per declared serving size in grams. Voluntary declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel; supports heart-healthy menu claims and nutritional transparency for guests.

Polyunsaturated fat content per declared serving size in grams. Voluntary declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel; included to support health-conscious menu positioning and nutritional transparency initiatives.

Potassium content per declared serving size in milligrams. Mandatory micronutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel per 2016 labeling rule update. Supports heart-health and blood-pressure-related menu claims.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(8)(v) – Potassium Declaration; FDA 2016 Nutrition Facts Rule

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this nutrition profile record, used in regulatory submissions, lab reports, and cross-system references (e.g., NP-BURG001). Distinct from the surrogate primary key.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101 – Food Labeling; NRA ServSafe Standards

description: Human-readable label for this nutrition profile, typically combining the menu item name and serving configuration (e.g., 'Classic Cheeseburger – Regular Serving'). Used in regulatory filings and internal reporting.

Classification of the nutrition profile indicating whether it represents a standard menu item, a customized build, a Limited Time Offer (LTO), a regional variant, or a test/pilot item. Drives which profiles are published to FDA-mandated menu boards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|customized|limited_time_offer|regional|test — promote to reference product if values expand]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101 – Food Labeling; FTC Menu Labeling Regulations

description: Protein content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory nutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Key metric for high-protein menu positioning and athletic/dietary segment marketing.

Saturated fat content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory sub-component of total fat on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Key metric for heart-health menu engineering and dietary guideline compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(2)(i) – Saturated Fat Declaration

description: Household measure description of the serving size as it appears on the FDA Nutrition Facts panel (e.g., '1 sandwich', '1 cup (240 mL)'). Required for consumer-facing menu labeling compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(b)(1) – Serving Size Declaration

description: Gram weight of the declared serving size as required by FDA Nutrition Facts panel standards. Used as the basis for all per-serving nutrient calculations and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(b)(1) – Serving Size Declaration

description: Sodium content per declared serving size in milligrams. Mandatory nutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. High-priority metric for menu reformulation initiatives, dietary guideline compliance, and public health reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(4) – Sodium Declaration; FDA Sodium Reduction Guidance

description: Soluble fiber content per declared serving size in grams. Voluntary sub-component of dietary fiber on FDA Nutrition Facts panel; supports heart-health and cholesterol-reduction menu claims.
Total carbohydrate content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory nutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Includes dietary fiber, total sugars, and added sugars as sub-components.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(6) – Total Carbohydrate Declaration

description: Total fat content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory nutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Includes saturated fat, trans fat, polyunsaturated fat, and monounsaturated fat.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(2) – Total Fat Declaration

description: Total sugars content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory sub-component of total carbohydrate on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Includes both naturally occurring and added sugars.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(6)(ii) – Total Sugars Declaration

description: Trans fat content per declared serving size in grams. Mandatory sub-component of total fat on FDA Nutrition Facts panel. Regulatory threshold: items with less than 0.5g may declare 0g per FDA rounding rules.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(2)(ii) – Trans Fat Declaration; FDA Trans Fat Final Rule

description: Sequential version number of this nutrition profile for the associated menu item. Incremented each time a recipe reformulation, ingredient substitution, or regulatory re-analysis results in a new nutrition profile. Supports historical audit trail and regulatory change management.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Vitamin D content per declared serving size in micrograms. Mandatory micronutrient declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts panel per 2016 labeling rule update. Expressed as micrograms (mcg) with percent Daily Value (%DV) calculated separately.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9(c)(8)(ii) – Vitamin D Declaration; FDA 2016 Nutrition Facts Rule

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each product mix (PMIX) record in the Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this transactional reporting entity.
Identifier of the fiscal period (week, period, or quarter) to which this PMIX record belongs, aligned to the restaurant's internal fiscal calendar. Supports period-over-period financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — CO Fiscal Calendar; GAAP/IFRS

description: Identifier of the menu item whose sales performance is captured in this PMIX record. Links to the menu item master entity.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Item Master

description: Identifier of the active marketing promotion or campaign associated with this menu item during the reporting period, if applicable. Links to the promotion master entity for campaign performance attribution.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Campaign Management; Oracle MICROS POS — Promotion Configuration

description: Identifier of the restaurant location for which this PMIX record was generated. Links to the restaurant master entity.
Identifier of the restaurant location for which this PMIX record was generated. Links to the restaurant master entity.

Average net selling price per unit of the menu item during the reporting period, calculated as net sales divided by units sold. Reflects actual realized price after discounts and may differ from the menu list price.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Price Analysis; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Accounting

description: Rank of the menu item within its menu category at the restaurant for the reporting period, ordered by units sold descending. Used to identify top and bottom performers within each category for menu rationalization decisions.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — PMIX Category Ranking; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Total Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the menu item during the reporting period, calculated as units sold multiplied by the item's standard recipe cost. Core input for menu engineering profitability analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — CO Cost Accounting; MarketMan Inventory Management — Recipe Costing; GAAP/IFRS

description: Cost of Goods Sold as a percentage of net sales (CoGS%) for the menu item during the reporting period. A key menu engineering metric used to classify items and assess profitability relative to category benchmarks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — CO Profitability Analysis; NRA Menu Engineering Standards; GAAP/IFRS

description: Total monetary value of complimentary (comped) menu items given during the reporting period. Represents a direct cost to the restaurant and is tracked for P&L accuracy and loss prevention.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Comp Tracking; SAP S/4HANA — Cost Accounting; GAAP/IFRS

description: Number of complimentary (comped) units of this menu item given to guests during the reporting period (e.g., for service recovery, employee meals, or promotional giveaways). Tracked for cost control and loss prevention.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Comp Tracking; NRA Operational Standards

description: Contribution margin for the menu item during the reporting period, calculated as net sales minus COGS. Represents the dollar contribution of the item toward covering fixed costs and generating profit. Core metric in menu engineering star/plow-horse/puzzle/dog classification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — CO Contribution Margin Analysis; NRA Menu Engineering Standards; GAAP/IFRS

description: Timestamp when this PMIX record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA monitoring for data pipeline freshness.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this PMIX record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for international restaurant operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA — Currency Configuration; GAAP/IFRS

description: Daypart segment during which the menu item sales are recorded (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner). Daypart is a core foodservice industry segmentation concept used for menu engineering, labor scheduling, and promotional analysis.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration; NRA Daypart Standards

description: Total value of discounts, promotional reductions, and coupon redemptions applied to the menu item during the reporting period. Used to reconcile gross to net sales and evaluate promotional effectiveness.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Discount Tracking; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Deductions; GAAP/IFRS

description: Total gross sales revenue generated by the menu item during the reporting period, before discounts, voids, and comps. Expressed in local currency. Core component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for revenue reporting.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Sales Summary; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Accounting; GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition

description: Indicates whether the menu item was available for sale during the entire reporting period at the restaurant. A value of False may indicate a stock-out, 86'd item, or temporary removal from the menu, which can suppress PMIX figures.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Item Availability; MarketMan Inventory Management — Stock Status

description: Indicates whether the menu item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO) during the reporting period. LTO items require separate PMIX tracking to evaluate promotional performance and inform future LTO decisions.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Item Attributes; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: The menu category to which the item belongs (e.g., Burgers, Beverages, Sides, Desserts, LTO). Used for category-level PMIX analysis and menu engineering decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entree|beverage|side|dessert|appetizer|lto|combo|condiment — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Category Master; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Menu engineering quadrant classification of the item for the reporting period based on the Boston Consulting Group (BCG) matrix adapted for foodservice: Star (high popularity, high margin), Plow Horse (high popularity, low margin), Puzzle (low popularity, high margin), Dog (low popularity, low margin). Core output of menu engineering analysis.
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; Kasavana & Smith Menu Engineering Methodology

description: The standard listed menu price for the item at the restaurant during the reporting period. Used to calculate price realization (average selling price vs. list price) and assess discount impact.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Price Configuration; FDA Menu Labeling Requirements

description: The percentage of total units sold at the restaurant during the reporting period that this menu item represents. Core Product Mix (PMIX) metric used to identify high-velocity and low-velocity items for menu engineering decisions.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — PMIX Report; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Net sales revenue for the menu item after deducting discounts, voids, and comps from gross sales. The primary revenue figure used in P&L reporting, PMIX analysis, and AUV calculations.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Net Sales; SAP S/4HANA — Net Revenue; GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition

description: Rank of the menu item across all menu items at the restaurant for the reporting period, ordered by units sold descending. Provides a holistic view of item velocity for menu engineering and operational prioritization.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — PMIX Overall Ranking; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Indicates whether the restaurant is company-owned or franchised. Enables separate PMIX benchmarking and menu engineering analysis across ownership models, which is critical for franchise royalty and P&L reporting.
reference: FranConnect — Franchise Classification; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations; IFA Standards

description: The externally-known report identifier or batch reference number from the Oracle MICROS POS system that is the source of this PMIX record. Enables traceability back to the originating POS report for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — PMIX Report Reference; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Lifecycle status of this PMIX record in the Silver layer. 'active' indicates the current valid record; 'revised' indicates the record has been superseded by a correction; 'voided' indicates the record was invalidated due to a data quality issue.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: Total monetary value of refunds issued for this menu item during the reporting period. Used in net revenue reconciliation and guest satisfaction analysis.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Refund Tracking; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Adjustments; GAAP/IFRS

description: Number of refunds issued for this menu item during the reporting period. Elevated refund counts may indicate quality issues, order accuracy problems, or guest dissatisfaction.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Refund Tracking; PCI DSS — Refund Transaction Controls

description: Calendar date for which this PMIX record applies. Used for daily and weekly menu engineering analysis and comparable store sales (Comp Sales / SSS) reporting.
reference: NRA Industry Reporting Standards; GAAP/IFRS Fiscal Period Alignment

description: Granularity of the PMIX record — whether it represents a daily, weekly, or daypart-level aggregation. Determines how units sold and revenue figures should be interpreted.
The restaurant service format (Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual, or fine-dining) associated with this PMIX record. Supports cross-format menu performance benchmarking.
reference: FranConnect — Restaurant Format Classification; NRA Industry Standards

description: The ordering channel through which the menu item was sold (e.g., Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), dine-in, kiosk). Enables channel-level PMIX analysis for menu engineering and pricing strategy.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; NRA Channel Reporting Standards

description: The percentage of total net sales revenue at the restaurant during the reporting period attributable to this menu item. Distinct from menu mix percentage (unit-based); this is revenue-based mix used for revenue contribution analysis.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Revenue Mix Report; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Analysis; NRA Standards

description: The Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code assigned to the menu item in the POS and inventory management systems. Used to reconcile PMIX data with supply chain and inventory records in MarketMan.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Item SKU; MarketMan Inventory Management — SKU Master

description: The operational system of record from which this PMIX record was sourced (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, or third-party delivery aggregator). Supports data lineage and reconciliation across channels.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Total number of hours during the reporting period that the menu item was unavailable for sale (e.g., due to stock-out or being 86'd). Used to adjust PMIX velocity calculations and identify supply chain or inventory issues.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Item 86 Tracking; MarketMan Inventory Management

description: Total number of individual menu item units sold during the reporting period at the restaurant. The primary volume metric in PMIX analysis and menu engineering.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Sales Transaction Data; NRA PMIX Reporting Standards

description: Timestamp when this PMIX record was last modified in the Silver layer, such as when a correction or revision was applied. Supports audit trail and change tracking for data governance.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: Total monetary value of voided transactions for this menu item during the reporting period. Used alongside void count for loss prevention analysis and operational quality monitoring.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Void Tracking; SAP S/4HANA — Revenue Adjustments

description: Number of voided transactions for this menu item during the reporting period. Voids indicate order errors, customer changes, or operational issues and are tracked separately from discounts for loss prevention and operational quality analysis.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Void Tracking; NRA Operational Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each item cost record. Primary key for the item_cost data product in the menu domain.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting assigns each menu item's calculated cost to a cost center for internal budgeting and variance analysis; essential for COGS reporting.
Reference to the menu item for which this theoretical cost record is calculated. Links to the menu.menu_item master product.
Reference to the specific recipe version (Bill of Materials) used to derive the theoretical cost. Ensures cost traceability to the exact ingredient composition at time of calculation.

business_glossary_term: Actual Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Actual CoGS%)
description: Actual realized Cost of Goods Sold percentage sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management, reflecting real purchase costs, waste, and yield losses. Compared against theoretical_cogs_pct to compute variance and identify operational inefficiencies.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Actual COGS Report; GAAP / IFRS

description: Name or employee identifier of the menu engineering or finance team member who approved this cost record for use in pricing decisions and P&L reporting. Required for SOX compliance and internal audit trails.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Internal Controls; SOX — Financial Reporting Controls

description: The date on which this item cost record was approved by the authorized reviewer. Used for audit trail and to establish the effective start of this cost record in menu engineering and P&L reporting.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Internal Controls; SOX — Financial Reporting Controls

description: The menu item's base selling price at the time of cost calculation, used as the denominator for CoGS% computation. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot to ensure historical cost percentage accuracy even when prices change.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Price Management; GAAP / IFRS

description: The sales channel for which this cost record applies: dine-in, Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), or all channels. Packaging and portion costs may differ by channel, resulting in channel-specific cost records.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS — Channel Management

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold Percentage Variance (CoGS% Variance)
description: Difference between actual_cogs_pct and theoretical_cogs_pct (actual minus theoretical). Positive variance indicates actual costs exceeded theoretical expectations, signaling waste, theft, or pricing issues. Critical metric for P&L reporting and operational control.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Variance Analysis; NRA Food Cost Management Standards

description: The business date on which the theoretical item cost was calculated. Used to track cost trends over time and align with menu engineering review cycles and P&L reporting periods.

Method used to derive the item cost: 'theoretical_bom' = derived from recipe Bill of Materials and price snapshot; 'actual_marktman' = sourced directly from MarketMan actual cost data; 'blended' = weighted combination of theoretical and actual. Ensures analytical transparency.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Cost Calculation; Internal COGS Methodology SOP

description: Theoretical cost per gram of the menu item (theoretical_cost_amount / portion_size_grams). Enables normalized cost comparison across items of different portion sizes and supports portion engineering decisions.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Lifecycle status of this item cost record. 'draft' indicates pending review; 'approved' indicates validated and active for menu engineering; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer calculation; 'archived' indicates retired from active use.
The timestamp when this item cost record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit creation trail for the Silver layer record.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP / IFRS — Audit Trail Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the cost amounts are expressed (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across international franchise markets.

The daypart for which this cost record applies (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, or all). Ingredient compositions and portion standards may vary by daypart, resulting in daypart-specific cost records for the same menu item.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration

description: The date on which this item cost record ceases to be active, typically when superseded by a new calculation. Null indicates the record is currently active. Supports bi-temporal cost history and trend analysis.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Period Cost Recognition; Internal Menu Engineering SOP

description: The date from which this item cost record is considered active and applicable for menu engineering, pricing, and P&L reporting. Supports bi-temporal modeling of cost history.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Period Cost Recognition; Internal Menu Engineering SOP

description: The fiscal accounting period (e.g., '2024-P04') to which this cost record is aligned for P&L reporting. Follows the company's internal fiscal calendar as managed in SAP S/4HANA. Enables period-over-period cost trend analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Cost Center Accounting; GAAP / IFRS — Period Reporting

description: Total number of distinct ingredients in the Bill of Materials (BOM) used for this cost calculation. Provides a quick indicator of recipe complexity and the number of price inputs contributing to the theoretical cost.

Indicates whether this cost record applies to a Limited Time Offer (LTO) menu item. LTO items often have unique cost structures due to specialty ingredients and require separate cost tracking for promotional P&L analysis.
reference: Internal LTO Management SOP; Oracle MICROS POS — LTO Configuration

description: The native record identifier from MarketMan Inventory Management for the actual cost data used in this calculation. Enables direct traceability back to the source system record for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Cost Record API

description: Menu engineering quadrant classification based on the item's profitability (CoGS%) and popularity (PMIX). 'star' = high popularity, high margin; 'plow_horse' = high popularity, low margin; 'puzzle' = low popularity, high margin; 'dog' = low popularity, low margin. Drives pricing and promotional strategy.
reference: NRA — Menu Engineering Framework (Kasavana & Smith); Internal Menu Engineering SOP

description: Free-text notes from the menu engineering or finance team explaining the rationale for this cost calculation, any manual adjustments made, or context for significant variances. Supports audit review and knowledge transfer.

Cost contribution of packaging materials (containers, wrappers, bags, cups) to the total theoretical item cost. Tracked separately from food ingredients to support packaging procurement decisions and sustainability reporting.
reference: Internal COGS Decomposition Standard; Coupa Procurement — Packaging Category

description: Standard portion size in grams for the menu item as defined in the recipe BOM at the time of cost calculation. Used to normalize cost-per-gram comparisons across menu items and to validate portion compliance with HACCP standards.
reference: FDA — Nutrition Labeling and Education Act (NLEA); HACCP — Portion Control CCP

description: The pricing basis applied to ingredient costs in this calculation: 'last_purchase' = most recent purchase price; 'moving_average' = weighted average of recent purchases; 'contract' = contracted supplier price from Coupa; 'standard' = standard cost set by finance. Affects cost comparability.
reference: Coupa Procurement — Contract Pricing; SAP S/4HANA MM — Standard Cost; GAAP / IFRS — Inventory Valuation

description: The effective date of the ingredient purchase prices used in this cost calculation. Distinct from cost_calculation_date — the snapshot may use prices from a prior period (e.g., last purchase price, rolling average). Critical for cost audit and variance explanation.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Price History; Coupa Procurement — Supplier Pricing

description: Reference to the ingredient price snapshot used in this cost calculation. Captures the specific point-in-time purchase prices from MarketMan that were applied to the Bill of Materials to derive theoretical Cost of Goods Sold (COGS).
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Purchase Order / Price History

description: Cost contribution of the primary protein ingredient(s) to the total theoretical item cost. Proteins (beef, chicken, seafood) are typically the highest-cost BOM component and are tracked separately for commodity price sensitivity analysis and USDA-aligned procurement reporting.
reference: USDA — Meat and Poultry Grading; Internal COGS Decomposition Standard

description: Version identifier of the recipe / Bill of Materials (BOM) used in this cost calculation (e.g., 'v3.2'). Enables traceability of cost changes driven by recipe reformulations versus ingredient price movements.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; ISO 22000 — Food Safety Management

description: The restaurant format for which this cost record applies: Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual dining, fine dining, or all formats. Cost structures differ by format due to labor, portion, and ingredient standards.
reference: Internal Restaurant Operations Standard; NRA — Restaurant Format Classification

description: The operational system of record from which this cost record originated: MarketMan (actual cost data), SAP S/4HANA (standard cost), Oracle MICROS POS (price data), or manual entry. Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; MarketMan Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA

business_glossary_term: Target Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Target CoGS%)
description: The budgeted or target CoGS% for this menu item as set by the finance and menu engineering teams. Used as the benchmark against which theoretical_cogs_pct and actual_cogs_pct are evaluated. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2800 = 28.00%).
reference: Internal Financial Planning SOP; GAAP / IFRS — Budget vs Actual Reporting

description: Acceptable tolerance band for CoGS% variance (theoretical vs actual) before an alert or review is triggered. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 = ±2.00%). Supports automated exception reporting in menu engineering workflows.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; GAAP / IFRS — Variance Tolerance Standards

business_glossary_term: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%)
description: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold expressed as a percentage of the menu item's base selling price (theoretical_cost_amount / base_price). Key menu engineering metric used to classify items and set pricing strategy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2850 = 28.50%).
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; GAAP / IFRS — Gross Margin Reporting

business_glossary_term: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Amount
description: The theoretical food cost amount in the operating currency for one unit of the menu item, derived by multiplying each Bill of Materials (BOM) ingredient quantity by its point-in-time purchase price. Core input to menu engineering and pricing decisions.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Cost of Goods Sold; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Absolute monetary variance between actual cost and theoretical cost per unit (actual cost amount minus theoretical_cost_amount). Expressed in the operating currency. Used alongside cogs_pct_variance for financial impact quantification in P&L reporting.

The timestamp when this item cost record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the most recent update for change management and audit trail purposes in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP / IFRS — Audit Trail Requirements

description: Theoretical food waste percentage factored into the cost calculation, representing expected yield loss during preparation (e.g., trim waste, cooking shrinkage). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5.00%). Sourced from recipe yield standards in MarketMan.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Waste Tracking; FDA — Food Waste Reduction Guidelines

description: Expected usable yield percentage of the primary ingredient after preparation (e.g., 0.8500 = 85% yield after trimming). Used in yield management calculations to adjust raw ingredient quantities in the BOM to achieve the target portion size.
reference: Internal Recipe Engineering Standard; NRA — Yield Management Best Practices

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each item price record in the menu pricing table. Primary key for the item_price data product.
Reference to the employee or user who approved this price record. Supports pricing governance and audit trail requirements.
Reference to the employee or user who approved this price record. Supports pricing governance and audit trail requirements.
Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee Pricing Audit Report tracks price overrides and royalty calculations per franchisee, requiring a direct FK.
Reference to the menu item for which this price record applies. Links the pricing record to the specific product being sold.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: Reference to the price tier classification (e.g., value, standard, premium) that governs the pricing band for this record. Supports tiered pricing strategies across restaurant formats.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: Reference to the specific restaurant location where this price is effective. Supports regional and location-level price variation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; FranConnect — Site Management

description: Reference to the specific restaurant location where this price is effective. Supports regional and location-level price variation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; FranConnect — Site Management

description: Current workflow state of the price record in the pricing governance process. Tracks whether the price is in draft, awaiting approval, approved for use, rejected, or superseded by a newer record.
reference: Internal Pricing Governance Policy; FTC Advertising Regulations

description: The date and time when this price record was formally approved by the authorized approver. Null when the record has not yet been approved. Supports pricing governance audit trail.
The standard selling price of the menu item before any promotional discounts, modifiers, or channel surcharges are applied. Represents the menu board price for the item at this restaurant.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; GAAP/IFRS

description: Additional amount added to the base price for specific ordering channels such as Third-Party Delivery (3PD) or Online Ordering (OLO) to offset platform fees or delivery costs. Zero when no surcharge applies.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS

description: The ratio of Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) to the base selling price, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.28 = 28%). A key menu engineering metric used to assess item profitability and pricing adequacy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — CO Module; NRA Industry Standards; GAAP/IFRS

description: The direct food and packaging cost associated with producing this menu item at the time of pricing. Used to calculate Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%) and support menu engineering decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — CO/MM Module; MarketMan Inventory Management; GAAP/IFRS

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which this price is applicable. Supports international pricing management across global restaurant operations.
The date and time when this price record was first created in the system. Audit field supporting data lineage and pricing history tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the price is denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency pricing for international restaurant operations.

The meal period or time segment during which this price is applicable. Daypart pricing enables different price points for the same item across Breakfast, Lunch, Afternoon, Dinner, and Late Night periods.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; NRA Industry Standards

description: The calendar date on which this price record expires and is no longer applied at the point of sale. Null for open-ended pricing records with no defined expiration.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; GAAP/IFRS

description: The calendar date on which this price record becomes active and the price is applied at the point of sale. Used to schedule future price changes and manage price transitions.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; GAAP/IFRS

description: The percentage deviation of the franchisee's actual price from the franchisor's Suggested Retail Price (SRP). Positive values indicate pricing above SRP; negative values indicate pricing below SRP. Used in franchise compliance monitoring.
reference: FranConnect — Franchise Compliance Tracking; IFA Franchise Best Practices; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations

description: Indicates whether this price record is currently active and eligible to be applied at the Point of Sale (POS). False for expired, superseded, or deactivated price records. Distinct from approval_status — a record may be approved but not yet active.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: Indicates whether this price record is associated with a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotion. True when the price is part of a time-bound promotional campaign; False for standard menu pricing.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; FTC Advertising Regulations

description: Indicates whether a cashier or manager is permitted to manually override this price at the Point of Sale (POS). Controls POS-level price override permissions for this item.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Internal POS Controls Policy

description: Alphanumeric identifier linking this price record to a specific Limited Time Offer (LTO) marketing campaign. Populated only when is_lto is True. Enables PMIX and revenue analysis by LTO campaign.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Salesforce CRM — Marketing Automation

description: The maximum number of units that can be ordered at this price point per transaction. Used to enforce promotional price limits and prevent abuse of discounted pricing.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; FTC Advertising Regulations

description: Menu engineering classification of the item based on its profitability and popularity (Product Mix / PMIX). Stars are high-profit/high-popularity; Plow Horses are low-profit/high-popularity; Puzzles are high-profit/low-popularity; Dogs are low-profit/low-popularity.
reference: NRA Industry Standards; Internal Menu Engineering Framework

description: The minimum number of units that must be ordered for this price to apply. Used for bulk pricing, catering orders, or combo deal minimum quantity thresholds.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: The sales channel for which this price record applies. Supports channel-specific pricing strategies across Dine-In, Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), Kiosk, and Catering.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Indicates whether the restaurant location is company-owned or franchised. Franchise pricing may be subject to different governance rules and royalty implications.
reference: FranConnect — Franchise Management; IFA Franchise Best Practices

description: The POS system price level code assigned to this price record in Oracle MICROS POS (e.g., PRICE_LEVEL_1, HAPPY_HOUR). Maps the price record to the POS configuration layer for terminal-level price application.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: Business justification or reason code explaining why this price was set or changed. Common reasons include commodity cost increase, competitive repositioning, LTO activation, regional adjustment, or franchise directive. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commodity_cost|competitive_repositioning|lto_activation|regional_adjustment|franchise_directive|menu_engineering|regulatory_compliance|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Internal Pricing Governance Policy; SAP S/4HANA — CO Module

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context or detail for the price change reason. Supplements the structured reason code with qualitative business commentary.
The formatted price string as it should appear on the menu board, digital display, or OLO/3PD platform (e.g., '$5.99', 'From $4.99'). Supports localized and channel-specific price presentation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; FDA Menu Labeling Regulations

description: Classification of the menu item's demand sensitivity to price changes. Used in menu engineering and revenue management to guide pricing decisions and promotional strategy.
reference: NRA Industry Standards; Internal Menu Engineering Framework

description: The maximum price value to which a POS operator may override this item's price when is_price_override_allowed is True. Null when price override is not permitted.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Internal POS Controls Policy

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this price record, used in POS configuration exports, pricing change communications, and audit references. Follows the format PRC-XXXXXXXX.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the geographic pricing region to which this price record belongs. Enables regional price variation management across markets (e.g., NORTHEAST, SOUTH, WEST, INTL).
reference: Internal Pricing Governance Policy; SAP S/4HANA — CO Module

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this price record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Oracle MICROS POS, SAP S/4HANA, Olo, and FranConnect.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; SAP S/4HANA; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; FranConnect

description: Sequential version number for this price record, incremented each time the price is updated. Supports price history tracking and audit trail requirements for the same item-restaurant-channel-daypart combination.
The discounted or special selling price applied during a promotional period or Limited Time Offer (LTO). Null when no active promotion applies to this item at this location.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; FTC Advertising Regulations

description: The restaurant service format to which this price applies. Supports format-specific pricing across Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), Casual Dining, Fine Dining, and Fast Casual establishments.
reference: NRA Industry Standards; FranConnect — Franchise Development

description: The native identifier of this price record in the originating operational system (e.g., MICROS POS price record ID, SAP condition record number). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; SAP S/4HANA — MM/SD Condition Records

description: The franchisor-recommended selling price for this menu item. For franchised locations, this is the corporate-suggested price that franchisees may adopt or deviate from within permitted bounds.
reference: FranConnect — Franchise Management; IFA Franchise Best Practices; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations

description: Code identifying the applicable tax category or tax class for this menu item price. Used by the POS system to apply the correct tax rate at the time of sale based on jurisdiction and item type.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; FDA Food Labeling Regulations; Local Tax Authorities

description: The date and time when this price record was last modified. Audit field supporting change tracking and data lineage for pricing governance.

Unique surrogate identifier for each standardized recipe record in the foodservice master recipe repository. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins to menu items, Bill of Materials (BOM) components, and production specifications.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Recipe development is performed by a chef; tracking the creator supports cost, quality, and compliance reporting.
Foreign key linking to foodsafety.allergen_profile. Business justification: SAFETY: Recipes inherit allergen control profiles that affect ingredient sourcing and labeling.
Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: QUALITY: Recipes have specific HACCP plans defining critical limits for preparation steps.

Reference to the parent menu item this recipe produces. Links the culinary production specification to the sellable menu item entity, enabling Product Mix (PMIX) analysis and menu engineering decisions.

Pipe-delimited list of major food allergens present in this recipe as required by FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA). Includes the FDA's Big 9 allergens: milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame. Used for menu labeling compliance and guest allergen communication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: milk|eggs|fish|shellfish|tree_nuts|peanuts|wheat|soybeans|sesame — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA FALCPA; FDA 21 CFR Part 101; ISO 22000

description: Name or employee identifier of the culinary or food safety authority who approved this recipe version for production use. Provides audit trail for recipe governance, food safety compliance, and SOP change management.
reference: ISO 22000; FDA FSMA; Internal SOP Governance

description: Date on which this recipe version was formally approved for production use by the culinary or food safety authority. Supports recipe version lifecycle management, audit trail, and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: ISO 22000; FDA FSMA; Internal SOP Governance

description: Supplementary Back of House (BOH) preparation notes and special handling instructions for kitchen staff, including equipment-specific guidance, batch preparation tips, and quality checkpoints not captured in the structured prep_method field. Sourced from culinary SOP documentation.

Total caloric content per serving in kilocalories (kcal) as required for FDA menu labeling compliance under the Affordable Care Act Section 4205. Displayed on menu boards and digital ordering platforms per FDA 21 CFR Part 101.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101; ACA Section 4205; USDA

description: Calories derived from fat content per serving in kilocalories (kcal), as declared on the FDA Nutrition Facts Panel. Supports nutritional transparency and menu labeling compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101; USDA Dietary Guidelines

description: The service channel(s) for which this recipe is approved and available. Supports channel-specific menu management across Dine-In, Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO via Olo platform), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), and Catering. Drives channel-specific menu configuration in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS

description: The primary cooking technique applied to this recipe (e.g., fry, grill, bake, steam, broil, saute, raw, microwave, sous_vide, smoke). Drives Kitchen Display System (KDS) routing, equipment assignment, and HACCP critical control point identification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fry|grill|bake|steam|broil|saute|raw|microwave|sous_vide|smoke — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: HACCP; FDA Food Code; NRA ServSafe; ISO 22000

description: Required internal or cooking equipment temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for this recipe. Critical for HACCP compliance, food safety critical control point (CCP) monitoring, and Zenput food safety audit verification. Ensures pathogen elimination per FDA Food Code minimum temperature requirements.
reference: HACCP; FDA Food Code; ISO 22000; NRA ServSafe

description: Standardized cook time in seconds for this recipe as defined in the culinary SOP. Used for Kitchen Display System (KDS) timer configuration, Speed of Service (SOS) benchmarking, Ticket Time analysis, and throughput optimization in BOH operations.
reference: Internal SOP Standards; Oracle MICROS KDS; NRA

description: Timestamp when this recipe record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data lineage, recipe version history, and compliance documentation.
reference: ISO 22000; Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail

description: The meal period or daypart during which this recipe is available for production and sale. Drives kitchen scheduling, labor forecasting via Planday, and daypart-level PMIX reporting. Standard foodservice dayparts include breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, snack, and all_day.
reference: NRA Industry Standards; Internal Menu Engineering

description: Date from which this recipe version becomes active and available for production in restaurant operations. Used for recipe version scheduling, Limited Time Offer (LTO) launch planning, and menu change management across Oracle MICROS POS and Olo.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Internal Menu Engineering; FTC Franchise Disclosure

description: Date on which this recipe version is retired from active production use. Applicable for Limited Time Offers (LTO), seasonal items, and superseded recipe versions. Null for open-ended core menu recipes.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Internal Menu Engineering

description: Calculated raw ingredient cost in USD to produce one yield unit of this recipe, derived from Bill of Materials (BOM) component costs in MarketMan Inventory Management. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) analysis, CoGS% reporting, menu pricing strategy, and P&L management in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; MarketMan Inventory Management; GAAP/IFRS

description: Recipe food cost expressed as a percentage of the menu selling price, representing the Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%). A key menu engineering metric used to evaluate recipe profitability, set pricing targets, and manage P&L performance. Calculated as food_cost divided by menu selling price.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; GAAP/IFRS; Internal Menu Engineering Standards

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point (HACCP) Flag
description: Indicates whether this recipe contains one or more Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP CCPs) requiring mandatory monitoring, verification, and corrective action procedures per ISO 22000 and FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA). Drives Zenput food safety audit task generation.
