description: Required hot or cold holding temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for maintaining food safety after cooking and prior to service. Critical HACCP critical control point (CCP) parameter monitored via Zenput food safety audits and local health department inspections.
reference: HACCP; FDA Food Code; ISO 22000; Local Health Departments

description: Maximum allowable holding time in minutes after cooking before the item must be discarded, per HACCP and food safety SOP. Drives waste tracking in MarketMan and Zenput task management for BOH discard compliance and Waste% reporting.
reference: HACCP; FDA Food Code; ISO 22000; NRA ServSafe

description: Indicates whether this recipe meets FDA gluten-free labeling standards (less than 20 ppm gluten). Used for menu labeling compliance, guest dietary accommodation, and marketing of gluten-free menu options per FDA 21 CFR Part 101.91.

Indicates whether this recipe contains no animal-derived ingredients, qualifying it as vegan. Supports menu labeling, dietary filtering on Olo Digital Ordering Platform, and guest dietary accommodation.
reference: NRA Menu Labeling Standards; Internal Menu Engineering

description: Indicates whether this recipe contains no meat, poultry, or seafood ingredients, qualifying it as vegetarian. Supports menu labeling, dietary filtering on Olo Digital Ordering Platform, and guest dietary accommodation.
reference: NRA Menu Labeling Standards; Internal Menu Engineering

description: Standard menu selling price in USD for this recipe as configured in Oracle MICROS POS. Used for revenue management, menu engineering analysis, Average Check Value (ACV) calculations, and Same-Store Sales (SSS) reporting.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; SAP S/4HANA AR; GAAP/IFRS

description: Standardized plating and presentation instructions for this recipe as defined in the culinary SOP. Specifies garnish placement, portioning, vessel type, and visual presentation standards for Front of House (FOH) service quality and brand consistency.

Standardized description of the preparation technique for this recipe as defined in the culinary Standard Operating Procedure (SOP). Includes steps such as marinating, chopping, mixing, or assembling performed prior to cooking. Governs BOH preparation workflow and kitchen training.
reference: NRA ServSafe; Internal SOP Standards; Zenput Operational Compliance

description: Standardized preparation time in seconds required before cooking begins, as defined in the culinary SOP. Contributes to total Ticket Time calculation and labor scheduling in Planday Workforce Scheduling for BOH staffing models.
reference: Internal SOP Standards; Planday Workforce Scheduling

description: Culinary category grouping for the recipe used in menu engineering and Product Mix (PMIX) reporting (e.g., Entrée, Appetizer, Dessert, Beverage, Side, Sauce, Condiment). reference: NRA Menu Labeling Standards; Internal Menu Engineering

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the recipe, used across Oracle MICROS POS, MarketMan Inventory Management, and SAP S/4HANA for cross-system reference. Follows the format RCP-XXXX. Serves as the operational lookup key in kitchen and procurement workflows.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; NRA ServSafe

description: Human-readable name of the standardized recipe as it appears in culinary Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) documentation and kitchen training materials. Used by Back of House (BOH) staff and culinary teams for identification and communication.

Current lifecycle state of the recipe within the culinary development and operations workflow. 'draft' indicates in-development; 'active' indicates approved for production use; 'inactive' indicates temporarily suspended; 'archived' indicates retired; 'under_review' indicates pending culinary or food safety re-evaluation.

Classification of the recipe by its menu permanence and scope. 'core' indicates a permanent menu item; 'limited_time_offer' (LTO) indicates a time-bound promotional item; 'seasonal' indicates availability tied to seasonal ingredients; 'test' indicates a recipe under culinary trial; 'regional' indicates a market-specific variant.

The restaurant format or concept for which this recipe is designed and approved. Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual, fine_dining, and fast_casual formats may have distinct recipe specifications, plating standards, and production methods.

Standardized serving size in grams as declared for nutritional labeling purposes. Required for FDA Nutrition Facts Panel compliance and menu labeling regulations. Used in nutritional data calculations and USDA reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101 Nutrition Labeling; USDA

description: Maximum shelf life in hours for the prepared recipe item from time of production, as defined in the food safety SOP. Governs discard scheduling, holding time compliance, and waste tracking in MarketMan. Critical for HACCP date-marking compliance per FDA Food Code.
reference: HACCP; FDA Food Code; ISO 22000; Local Health Departments

description: Sodium content per serving in milligrams (mg) as declared on the FDA Nutrition Facts Panel. Critical for menu labeling compliance, nutritional transparency, and alignment with USDA Dietary Guidelines sodium reduction targets.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101; USDA Dietary Guidelines; ACA Section 4205

description: Required storage temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for raw ingredients or prepared components of this recipe prior to production. HACCP critical control point (CCP) parameter for cold chain compliance, monitored via Zenput food safety audits.
reference: HACCP; FDA Food Code; ISO 22000; Local Health Departments

description: Secondary culinary classification providing finer granularity within the recipe category (e.g., Grilled, Fried, Baked, Raw, Blended). Used for BOH workflow routing, kitchen station assignment, and menu engineering analysis.

Total elapsed time in seconds from start of preparation to completion of plating, combining prep_time_seconds and cook_time_seconds plus any resting or holding time. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) targets, throughput modeling, and KDS configuration.
reference: Internal SOP Standards; Oracle MICROS KDS

description: Timestamp when this recipe record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, recipe version audit trail, and Silver Layer data freshness monitoring in the Databricks Lakehouse.

Version number of the recipe in major.minor format (e.g., 1.0, 2.3), tracking iterative culinary changes, reformulations, or ingredient substitutions over the recipe lifecycle. Enables version-controlled recipe management and rollback capability.
reference: ISO 22000 Food Safety Management Systems; Internal SOP Standards

description: Expected food waste percentage for this recipe during production, representing trim loss, cooking loss, and preparation waste as a proportion of raw ingredient weight. Used for Yield Management, PAR Level planning, and Waste% reporting in MarketMan Inventory Management.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management; USDA; Internal SOP Standards

description: The standardized output quantity produced by one execution of this recipe (e.g., 1.0 for a single portion, 12.0 for a batch of 12 units). Used in Bill of Materials (BOM) scaling, food cost calculations, and PAR Level (Periodic Automatic Replenishment Level) planning in MarketMan.
reference: ISO 22000; Internal SOP Standards; MarketMan Inventory Management

description: Unit of measure for the recipe yield quantity (e.g., portion, oz, g, kg, lb, ml, L, each, batch, serving). reference: MarketMan Inventory Management; USDA; Internal SOP Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Bill of Materials (BOM) line-item record linking an ingredient to a recipe. Primary key for the recipe_ingredient entity.
Reference to the master ingredient or Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) record that identifies the specific raw material, semi-finished, or finished component used in this recipe line.
Reference to the approved substitute ingredient SKU that may replace this ingredient when is_substitution_allowed is True. Supports supply chain contingency planning and procurement flexibility.

description: Reference to the preferred or approved supplier for this ingredient as maintained in the Coupa Procurement supplier master. Used for procurement planning, spend analytics, and supplier performance management.

description: Reference to the parent recipe to which this ingredient belongs. Establishes the Bill of Materials (BOM) header-to-line relationship.

Pipe-delimited list of major food allergens present in this ingredient as required by FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA) and EU Regulation 1169/2011 (e.g., 'gluten|milk|eggs|soy'). Drives menu allergen declarations and guest-facing nutritional disclosures.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); EU Regulation 1169/2011; ISO 22000

description: Name or employee identifier of the culinary or quality assurance authority who approved this ingredient for use in the recipe. Supports recipe governance, food safety audit trails, and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: Internal Recipe Governance Policy; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); ISO 22000

description: Date on which this ingredient was formally approved for inclusion in the recipe by the designated culinary or quality assurance authority. Required for food safety compliance documentation and recipe change control audit trails.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); ISO 22000; Internal Recipe Governance Policy

description: Version identifier for the recipe Bill of Materials (BOM) line, following semantic versioning convention (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Enables change control tracking, regulatory audit trails, and rollback capability for recipe modifications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — BOM Change Management; Internal Recipe Governance Policy

description: Indicates whether this ingredient contains milk or dairy derivatives as a major allergen per FDA FALCPA. True = dairy present; False = dairy-free. Supports allergen management and guest dietary accommodation.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA)

description: Indicates whether this ingredient contains or may contain gluten as a major allergen per FDA FALCPA requirements. True = gluten present or cross-contact risk; False = gluten-free. Used for menu allergen labeling and guest dietary accommodation.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); FDA Gluten-Free Labeling Rule (21 CFR 101.91)

description: Procurement cost of the ingredient per one unit of measure at the time of recipe costing. Used as the primary input for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) and Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%) calculations at the recipe level. Sourced from Coupa Procurement or SAP AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Product Costing; Coupa Procurement; GAAP/IFRS Cost Accounting

description: Timestamp when this recipe ingredient BOM line record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for recipe development and change management.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which ingredient cost values (cost_per_unit, extended_cost) are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global franchise markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting Standards

description: Date on which this ingredient version expires or is superseded within the recipe Bill of Materials (BOM). Null indicates the ingredient is currently active with no planned end date. Supports LTO lifecycle management and recipe change control.
reference: Internal Recipe Versioning Policy; SAP S/4HANA MM — BOM Validity

description: Date from which this ingredient version is active and valid within the recipe Bill of Materials (BOM). Supports recipe versioning, Limited Time Offer (LTO) ingredient management, and historical COGS analysis.
reference: Internal Recipe Versioning Policy; SAP S/4HANA MM — BOM Validity

description: Total cost contribution of this ingredient line to the recipe, calculated as quantity × cost_per_unit adjusted for yield. Represents the line-level Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) input for recipe costing and menu engineering decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Product Costing; GAAP/IFRS Cost Accounting

description: Indicates whether this ingredient represents a Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point (HACCP) in the recipe preparation process requiring mandatory temperature, time, or handling controls. True = CCP applies; False = standard handling. Drives food safety audit requirements in Zenput.
reference: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points); FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); ISO 22000; Zenput Food Safety Audits

description: Current lifecycle status of this ingredient within the recipe Bill of Materials (BOM). Controls whether the ingredient is actively used in production, pending approval, seasonal, or discontinued. Drives menu engineering and procurement decisions.
reference: Internal Menu Governance Policy; MarketMan Inventory Management

description: Indicates whether this ingredient is designated as a critical or signature component of the recipe whose absence or substitution would materially alter the menu item's identity, quality standard, or brand promise. True = critical; False = non-critical. Drives supply chain risk prioritization.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; NRA Quality Standards

description: Indicates whether this ingredient holds a valid Halal certification from an accredited certifying body. True = Halal certified; False = not certified. Required for menu compliance in markets with Halal dietary requirements.

description: Indicates whether this ingredient holds a valid Kosher certification. True = Kosher certified; False = not certified. Required for menu compliance in markets with Kosher dietary requirements.

description: Indicates whether this ingredient is certified organic under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) standards. True = USDA Organic certified; False = conventional. Supports menu labeling claims and premium ingredient sourcing tracking.

description: Indicates whether this ingredient may be substituted with an approved alternative ingredient without altering the recipe's quality, taste profile, or regulatory compliance. True = substitution permitted; False = ingredient is mandatory and cannot be substituted.

description: Ordinal position of this ingredient within the recipe Bill of Materials (BOM). Governs the assembly and preparation sequence displayed on Kitchen Display System (KDS) and Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) documentation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — KDS Configuration; Internal SOP Standards

description: Minimum required internal cooking temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for this ingredient as mandated by FDA Food Code and HACCP plan. Applicable when haccp_critical_control_point is True. Enforced via Zenput food safety task management.

description: Free-text field for additional preparation instructions, handling notes, quality specifications, or sourcing requirements specific to this ingredient within the recipe (e.g., 'slice to 1/4 inch thickness', 'use Grade A only', 'apply after cooking'). Supports kitchen SOP documentation.
reference: Internal SOP Standards; NRA ServSafe Certification

description: Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) level quantity for this ingredient at the restaurant level, expressed in the ingredient's unit of measure. Drives automated reorder triggers in MarketMan and procurement planning in Coupa.

description: Preparation state of the ingredient at the point of use in the recipe (e.g., raw, cooked, prepped, frozen, thawed). Drives kitchen workflow sequencing, food safety HACCP critical control point documentation, and yield calculations. reference: HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points); FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); Zenput Food Safety Audits

description: Specified quantity of the ingredient required per one unit of the parent recipe, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Used as the basis for COGS calculations, procurement planning, and PAR level management.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA MM — BOM

description: Maximum number of days this ingredient remains safe and usable from the date of receipt or preparation, under specified storage conditions. Used for PAR level management, waste reduction, and FIFO inventory rotation in MarketMan.
reference: FDA Food Code; MarketMan Inventory Management — Shelf Life Tracking; HACCP

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for this ingredient. Exceeding this threshold triggers a food safety violation per HACCP plan and FDA Food Code requirements.
Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for this ingredient to maintain food safety and quality standards. Used in HACCP temperature monitoring and cold chain compliance tracking.

The supplier's own item or catalogue code for this ingredient, as used on purchase orders and delivery documentation. Enables cross-referencing between internal SKU and supplier catalogue for procurement and receiving.

description: Unit of measure in which the ingredient quantity is expressed (e.g., oz, g, ml, each). reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Unit of Measure; USDA Measurement Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this recipe ingredient BOM line record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, data lineage, and Silver Layer incremental processing in Databricks.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: Percentage of the ingredient quantity expected to be lost during preparation, trimming, cooking, or handling (e.g., 5.00 = 5%). Applied in COGS calculations and procurement planning to account for Food Waste Percentage (Waste%). Sourced from MarketMan waste tracking.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Waste Tracking; NRA Food Waste Guidelines

description: Percentage of the as-purchased ingredient quantity that results in usable product after preparation (e.g., 80.00 = 80% yield). Complements waste_factor_pct and is used in Yield Management calculations to determine actual cost per usable unit.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management; USDA Yield Factors for Food Calculations

description: Unique identifier for the asset depreciation posting record. Primary key for the asset_depreciation product.
Reference to the fixed asset being depreciated. Links to the fixed asset master record in SAP FI-AA.
Reference to the franchise entity that owns or operates the asset. Null for company-owned assets. Supports franchise vs. company-owned asset segmentation.
Reference to the restaurant location where the asset is deployed. Supports location-level asset tracking and P&L allocation for restaurant operations.
Reference to the restaurant location where the asset is deployed. Supports location-level asset tracking and P&L allocation for restaurant operations.
Total accumulated depreciation for this asset in this depreciation area as of the end of this fiscal period. Cumulative sum of all prior depreciation postings.
Original acquisition or construction cost of the fixed asset in this depreciation area. Basis for depreciation calculation.
Date on which the fixed asset was acquired or placed in service. Determines the start of depreciation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Asset class code categorizing the fixed asset (e.g., building, kitchen equipment, furniture, vehicles, leasehold improvements). Determines default depreciation parameters and GL account assignments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Asset Class Configuration

description: Short textual description of the fixed asset (e.g., 'Fryer - Kitchen Station 3', 'POS Terminal - Front Counter'). Provides business context for the asset.
Date on which the fixed asset was retired, sold, or scrapped. Null if the asset is still in service. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Manufacturer serial number or unique identifier for the physical asset. Used for equipment tracking and warranty management.
Four-character company code identifying the legal entity for which depreciation is posted. Aligns with SAP organizational structure.
Cost center to which the depreciation expense is allocated. Supports restaurant-level or department-level P&L reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting

description: User ID of the person or system process that created this depreciation record. Supports audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when this depreciation record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record. Aligns with the company code currency.
Periodic depreciation expense amount posted for this asset in this fiscal period and depreciation area. Expressed in the company code currency.

Depreciation area code indicating the valuation view: 01=Book Depreciation (GAAP), 10=Tax Depreciation, 15=IFRS, 20=Cost Accounting, 30=Consolidated, 40=Group Valuation. Supports parallel accounting requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Depreciation Areas; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16

description: Four-character code defining the depreciation calculation method (e.g., straight-line, declining balance, units of production). Controls depreciation calculation logic in SAP FI-AA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Depreciation Key Configuration; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16

description: Business-friendly name of the depreciation calculation method applied. Derived from depreciation_key for reporting clarity.
Date on which the depreciation calculation batch job was executed. May differ from posting_date in case of backdated runs. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Status of the depreciation posting: posted=successfully posted to GL, planned=calculated but not yet posted, reversed=posting reversed due to correction, adjusted=manual adjustment applied.
SAP FI document number for the depreciation posting. Links to the GL document for audit trail and drill-down.
Fiscal period (1-12 or 1-13 for special periods) within the fiscal year for which depreciation is posted. Supports monthly and period-end close processes.
Four-digit fiscal year for which the depreciation is posted. Aligns with the restaurant's financial reporting calendar.
GL account number to which the accumulated depreciation is credited. Contra-asset account on the balance sheet.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts; GAAP ASC 360

description: GL account number to which the depreciation expense is debited. Typically a P&L expense account.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts; GAAP ASC 360

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the asset has been tested for impairment and an impairment loss has been recognized. True if impaired, False otherwise.
Cumulative impairment loss recognized for this asset in this depreciation area. Null if no impairment has been recorded.
User ID of the person or system process that last modified this depreciation record. Supports audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when this depreciation record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Net book value of the asset as of the end of this fiscal period. Calculated as acquisition_value minus accumulated_depreciation. Represents the carrying amount on the balance sheet.
Date on which the depreciation transaction was posted to the general ledger. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Remaining useful life of the asset in years as of the end of this fiscal period. Calculated as useful_life_years minus elapsed depreciation periods.
SAP FI document number of the reversal posting if this depreciation has been reversed. Null if not reversed.
Boolean flag indicating whether this depreciation posting has been reversed. True if reversed, False otherwise.
Estimated useful life of the asset in years as configured in the depreciation key for this depreciation area. Used for straight-line and other time-based depreciation methods.
Unique identifier for the capital expenditure project record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Needed for capital‑expenditure planning linking each capex project to the specific facility it upgrades, used in CAPEX approval and facility budgeting processes.
Foreign key to the franchise entity if this CapEx project is franchise-funded or co-funded. Null for company-owned projects.
Foreign key to the restaurant location this CapEx project is associated with (for NRO, remodel, or site-specific equipment projects). Null for corporate-level technology or multi-site programs.
Foreign key to the restaurant location this CapEx project is associated with (for NRO, remodel, or site-specific equipment projects). Null for corporate-level technology or multi-site programs.
Actual date when the project was capitalized and assets placed in service. Null if not yet capitalized.
Actual date when project work was completed and assets were ready for use. Null if project is still in progress.
Cumulative actual expenditures posted to the project to date. Includes invoiced costs, accruals, and internal labor capitalized.
Name or title of the individual or committee that approved the project (e.g., CFO, CapEx Committee, Board of Directors).
Date when the CapEx project was formally approved by the governance committee or authorized signatory.
Total capital budget approved for this project by the CapEx governance committee or executive leadership. Represents the authorized spending limit.
Fixed asset class that will receive the capitalized costs. Determines depreciation method and useful life.

business_glossary_term: Asset Under Construction (AUC) General Ledger (GL) Account
description: General ledger account number for the Asset Under Construction balance sheet line item. Costs accumulate here until capitalization.
Business case rationale for the capital investment, including strategic alignment, expected benefits, and financial analysis.
Explanation for project cancellation if status is cancelled. Null for active or completed projects.

Strategic classification of the CapEx investment. Growth = revenue expansion, maintenance = asset replacement, compliance = regulatory requirement, efficiency = cost reduction, strategic = competitive positioning.
Total committed costs via purchase orders and contracts not yet invoiced. Used for budget consumption tracking and forecasting.
SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for this CapEx project. Links to finance.legal_entity for consolidation and reporting.
Cost center responsible for project oversight and ongoing asset maintenance post-capitalization.
User ID or name of the person who created this project record.
Timestamp when this CapEx project record was first created in the system.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this project record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Depreciation method to be applied once the asset is capitalized (e.g., straight_line, declining_balance).
Planned date when the project will be capitalized and transferred from AUC to fixed assets, triggering depreciation start.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this project record.
Timestamp when this CapEx project record was last modified.
Free-form notes and comments for additional context, issues, or special considerations.
Expected number of months to recover the capital investment through incremental cash flows or cost savings.
Target date for project completion as defined in the project plan.
Profit center to which this CapEx project is assigned. Used for segment reporting and P&L allocation of depreciation expense.
Detailed narrative description of the project scope, objectives, and deliverables.
Corporate email address of the project manager for communication and escalation.
Full name of the project manager responsible for execution and delivery.
Human-readable name of the capital project (e.g., 'NRO - Dallas Market Street', 'Remodel - Chicago Loop Kitchen Upgrade').
Business identifier for the CapEx project as assigned in SAP PS. Externally visible project code used in financial reporting and approvals.

Current phase of the project lifecycle. Initiation = business case development, design = architectural/engineering, procurement = vendor selection, construction = build-out, commissioning = testing and handover, closeout = final accounting and asset transfer.
Official start date of the project, typically when budget is approved and work begins.

Current lifecycle status of the CapEx project. Planning = under development, approved = budget authorized, in_progress = construction/implementation underway, on_hold = temporarily suspended, completed = work finished, cancelled = terminated before completion, closed = financially closed and capitalized. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planning|approved|in_progress|on_hold|completed|cancelled|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the capital project. NRO = New Restaurant Opening, remodel = major renovation, equipment_replacement = asset refresh, technology_infrastructure = IT/POS systems, maintenance_capex = major repairs capitalized, facility_expansion = capacity additions.
Calculated remaining budget: approved_budget_amount minus (actual_spend_amount + committed_cost_amount). Indicates available funds.
Overall risk assessment for the project based on complexity, budget size, timeline, and dependencies. Used for governance oversight.
Target return on investment percentage established during project approval. Used for post-implementation performance evaluation.
Executive sponsor or business owner accountable for project outcomes and ROI realization.
Expected useful life in years for depreciation purposes once the asset is capitalized.
Budget variance: actual_spend_amount minus approved_budget_amount. Positive indicates over-budget, negative indicates under-budget.
Budget variance as a percentage of approved budget: (variance_amount / approved_budget_amount) * 100.
Unique identifier for the fixed asset record. Primary key sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AA asset master.
Identifier of the vendor or supplier from whom the asset was purchased. Links to vendor master for procurement and warranty tracking.
Identifier of the employee or manager responsible for the custody and maintenance of the asset. Supports accountability and asset stewardship.
Identifier of the restaurant location or corporate facility where the asset is physically located. Links to location master for site-level asset reporting.
Identifier of the vendor or supplier from whom the asset was purchased. Links to vendor master for procurement and warranty tracking.
Total depreciation expense recognized to date since acquisition. Contra-asset account that reduces the gross book value to net book value.
Total capitalized cost of the fixed asset at acquisition including purchase price, installation, freight, and other directly attributable costs. Recorded in USD.
Date when the fixed asset was acquired or placed into service. Used as the start date for depreciation calculations and useful life tracking.
Classification category of the fixed asset used for grouping and reporting. Determines depreciation rules and capitalization thresholds per GAAP/IFRS standards.
reference: GAAP ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; IFRS IAS 16

description: Detailed business description of the fixed asset including make, model, and specifications (e.g., 'Vulcan 6-Burner Gas Range Model XYZ123').
Externally-known unique asset tag or serial number assigned to the fixed asset for tracking and identification purposes. Used for physical asset verification and audit trails.

Current lifecycle status of the fixed asset. Determines whether depreciation is active and whether the asset appears on the balance sheet. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|in_service|under_construction|retired|disposed|impaired|held_for_sale — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Detailed sub-classification within the asset class for granular tracking (e.g., 'Fryers', 'Grills', 'KDS Units' under kitchen_equipment).
Reference code linking the asset to the originating capital expenditure project or budget line item. Supports CapEx tracking and ROI analysis.
SAP cost center code to which depreciation expense is allocated. Supports P&L reporting by organizational unit and enables labor% and CoGS% analysis.

description: Timestamp when the fixed asset record was first created in the SAP S/4HANA system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost and book value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency consolidation for international operations.
Accounting method used to calculate periodic depreciation expense. Straight-line is most common for restaurant equipment; declining balance may be used for vehicles.
Date when the asset was retired, sold, or otherwise disposed of. Triggers final depreciation calculation and removal from active asset register.
Method by which the asset was disposed of. Determines accounting treatment and tax implications for gain/loss recognition.
Cash or fair value received from the sale or disposal of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal for P&L reporting.
General ledger account code for the asset account in the chart of accounts. Used for financial statement preparation and GAAP/IFRS compliance.

description: Flag indicating whether the asset has been tested for impairment and found to have a carrying value exceeding its recoverable amount per GAAP/IFRS standards.
Amount of impairment loss recognized when the asset's carrying value exceeds its fair value or value in use. Recorded as a non-cash charge to P&L.
Policy number for property and casualty insurance covering the asset. Used for claims processing and risk management.
Timestamp when the fixed asset record was last updated in the SAP S/4HANA system. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
Date of the most recent physical verification or audit of the asset. Supports SOX compliance and internal control over fixed assets.
Flag indicating whether the asset is subject to a lease agreement (finance lease or operating lease under ASC 842 / IFRS 16). Determines right-of-use asset treatment.
Name of the equipment manufacturer or brand (e.g., Vulcan, Hobart, NCR). Supports vendor performance analysis and standardization initiatives.

Indicates whether this cost center is eligible to receive capital expenditure allocations. True for restaurant units (equipment, R&M), distribution centers, and major corporate facilities. False for pure administrative departments. Used to distinguish CapEx from OpEx in financial reporting.

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold Percent (COGS%) Target
description: The target COGS% (cost of goods sold as a percentage of revenue) for this cost center. Critical KPI for food cost management. Typical QSR targets range from 25-35%. Used for menu engineering, pricing strategy, and supplier negotiations.

The SAP company code (legal entity) to which this cost center belongs. Used for GAAP/IFRS financial statement consolidation and statutory reporting. Company-owned units vs franchise entities are distinguished here.

The SAP Controlling Area to which this cost center belongs. Controlling areas represent the highest level of cost accounting organization, typically aligned with legal entities or major geographic regions for multi-entity consolidation.

High-level categorization of the cost center's role. Revenue-generating centers (FOH, BOH) directly contribute to sales; support centers (maintenance, training) enable operations; administrative centers (HR, finance) provide governance; overhead centers (facilities, utilities) are indirect; capital project centers track CapEx initiatives.
The externally-known alphanumeric code for the cost center as defined in SAP S/4HANA Controlling module. Used in financial reporting and P&L statements.
Free-text description providing additional context about this cost center's purpose, scope, or special characteristics. Used for documentation and reporting clarity.
The full business name of the cost center (e.g., 'Store #1234 - Downtown Chicago', 'Regional Office - Midwest', 'Corporate HR Department').

Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers accept cost postings; inactive centers are temporarily disabled; pending centers are awaiting approval; closed centers are permanently retired; suspended centers are on hold; planned centers are scheduled for future activation (e.g., NRO pipeline).

Classification of the cost center by organizational function. Restaurant units are individual QSR locations; regional offices manage geographic territories; corporate departments are headquarters functions; shared service centers provide centralized support; distribution centers handle supply chain; franchise support manages franchisee relations.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this cost center operates. Used for multi-country consolidation, currency translation, and jurisdiction-specific compliance (FDA, USDA, local health departments).
The user ID of the person who created this cost center record. Used for audit trails and accountability.
The timestamp when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for this cost center's local currency. Used for budget planning, cost allocation, and multi-currency consolidation in GAAP/IFRS financial statements.
The number of drive-thru lanes at this location. Nullable for non-DT formats. Critical for SOS (Speed of Service) analysis, throughput capacity planning, and DT-specific cost allocation.

The restaurant format classification. Examples: DT (Drive-Thru), DINE_IN, KIOSK, FOOD_COURT, AIRPORT, GHOST_KITCHEN. Used for format-specific cost benchmarking, throughput analysis, and real estate site selection.

Indicates whether this cost center represents a franchise-owned unit (true) or a company-owned unit (false). Critical for revenue recognition (royalty income vs direct sales), P&L consolidation, and franchise compliance tracking.
The functional area classification for cross-company-code reporting. Typical values include operations, marketing, R&D, administration, IT, finance. Used for functional P&L views and OpEx analysis.

The target Labor% (labor cost as a percentage of revenue) for this cost center. Critical KPI for restaurant operations. Typical QSR targets range from 20-30%. Used for workforce scheduling optimization and P&L performance evaluation.
The user ID of the person who last modified this cost center record. Used for audit trails and change accountability.
The timestamp when this cost center record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness validation.
Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or historical context. Examples: 'Undergoing renovation Q2 2024', 'Pilot location for new POS system', 'Seasonal closure Nov-Mar'.

The method used to allocate shared operating expenses to this cost center. Direct costs are posted directly; allocated methods distribute corporate overhead based on revenue, headcount, square footage, transaction volume, or fixed percentages. Used for full-absorption P&L reporting.
The cost center code of the immediate parent in the organizational hierarchy. Nullable for top-level cost centers. Enables recursive hierarchy traversal for regional and corporate rollups.

The profit center assignment for this cost center. Profit centers are used for internal P&L reporting and EBITDA tracking. Restaurant units typically map 1:1 to profit centers; corporate departments may roll up to shared profit centers.
The geographic region code for this cost center. Used for regional P&L rollups, SSS (Same-Store Sales) analysis, and regional manager accountability. Examples: NEAST, SWEST, MIDWEST, INTL.

The number of customer seats available at this cost center location. Nullable for non-dine-in formats (drive-thru only, ghost kitchens). Used for cover count analysis, table turn calculations, and throughput benchmarking.
The total interior square footage of this cost center facility. Used for OpEx allocation (utilities, CAM charges), productivity metrics (sales per square foot), and real estate valuation.

The date from which this cost center becomes effective and can accept cost postings. Aligns with NRO (New Restaurant Opening) dates for restaurant units or organizational change effective dates for corporate centers.
The date through which this cost center remains effective. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Populated when a restaurant closes, a department is dissolved, or a regional office is consolidated.
Unique identifier for the financial period record. Primary key.
Foreign key reference to the immediately preceding period (e.g., prior month). Used for sequential period-over-period analysis.
Foreign key reference to the equivalent period in the prior fiscal year. Used for year-over-year comparative analysis and SSS reporting.

Two-character code identifying the ledger (leading ledger for primary GAAP, non-leading ledger for parallel IFRS, special purpose ledger for management reporting). Supports multi-GAAP parallel accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Universal Journal, IFRS IAS 1

description: Self-referencing FK on financial_period (prior_financial_period_id)

Accounting standard applied in this ledger: US_GAAP (United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), local_GAAP (country-specific standards), management (internal reporting).
Indicates whether adjustment postings (corrections, reclassifications) are allowed in this period after initial close. Used for controlled reopening during audit or correction cycles.
Reason code or description for why the period is an adjustment period.
Indicates whether this period is subject to external audit review. Typically true for year-end periods and quarterly periods for publicly traded entities.
True if changes to this period are tracked for audit purposes.
Total number of calendar days in the period. Used for daily average calculations and period normalization in financial analysis.
Month number (1‑12) in the calendar year.
Quarter number (1‑4) in the calendar year.
Gregorian calendar year of the period.
Actual date when the period-end close process was completed and the period status changed to closed. Null if period is still open.
Target date for completing the period-end close process. Used for close calendar planning and tracking adherence to financial reporting deadlines.
Number of days between scheduled and actual close date (positive if late, negative if early). Used to track period-end close efficiency and identify process improvement opportunities.
Four-character alphanumeric code identifying the legal entity in SAP S/4HANA. Links this period to a specific company within the multi-entity structure.
Code identifying the consolidation group for multi‑entity reporting.
Indicates whether this period requires multi-entity consolidation processing. True for periods where intercompany eliminations and group reporting are performed.
User ID or username of the person who created this financial period record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when this financial period record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Total count of calendar days covered by the period.
Date when the period ceases to be effective (nullable).
Date when the period becomes effective for reporting purposes.
Last calendar day of the financial period.
Conversion rate from functional currency to reporting currency for the period.

Type of exchange rate applied for currency translation in this period: average (period average rate for P&L), closing (period-end rate for balance sheet), budget (planning rate), historical (acquisition date rate for fixed assets).
Optional free‑text description of the period.
Type of financial statement the period primarily supports.
Month number (1‑12) within the fiscal year.
Standard code used by SAP for the fiscal period (e.g., "2023P01").
Quarter number (1‑4) within the fiscal year.
Four-digit fiscal year to which this period belongs. May differ from calendar year depending on fiscal year variant configuration.

Two-character code defining the fiscal calendar structure (number of periods, special periods, year-end rules) used by the company code. Determines how the fiscal year is divided into posting periods.
Degree to which holidays affect the period.
True if the period is used for post‑close adjustments.
True if this period is the active reporting period.
True if the period values are based on estimates rather than actuals.
True if the period includes a recognized holiday schedule.
True if the period is an interim (non‑full) reporting period.
Classification of the ledger: leading (primary GAAP ledger for statutory reporting), non_leading (parallel IFRS or local GAAP ledger), special_purpose (management or segment reporting ledger).
True when the period is locked for posting to prevent changes.
User ID or username of the person who last modified this financial period record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Timestamp when this financial period record was last modified. Updated whenever period status, close dates, or other attributes change.
Free-text field for recording period-specific notes, exceptions, or special circumstances (e.g., 'Includes acquisition of XYZ brand', 'Impacted by COVID-19 closures', 'Leap year adjustment').
Business classification of the period data.
Last calendar date of the financial period. Defines the end of the posting window for transactions assigned to this period.
Compact code representing the period (e.g., FY2023Q1, FY2023M04).
Human-readable name or label for the period (e.g., 'January 2024', 'Q1 FY2024', 'Period 13 - Year-End Adjustments'). Used for reporting and user interface display.
Sequential period number within the fiscal year (typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 for 4-4-5 retail calendars). Special periods (13, 14, 15, 16) used for year-end adjustments.
Sequential order of the period within its fiscal year.
First calendar date of the financial period. Defines the beginning of the posting window for transactions assigned to this period.

Current posting status of the period: not_opened (future period, no posting allowed), open (active posting allowed), closed (period-end close completed, limited posting), locked (archived, no posting allowed).
Classification of the period: regular (standard posting period), special (year-end adjustment period 13-16), or adjustment (mid-year correction period).
Indicates whether new transactions can be posted to this period. True if period is open for posting, false if closed or locked.
Fiscal quarter number (1-4) to which this period belongs. Supports quarterly financial reporting and Same-Store Sales (SSS) analysis.
reference: GAAP Financial Reporting, SEC Regulation S-X

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for consolidated financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro). Used for multi-currency consolidation.
True if the period is used for segment‑level reporting.
Originating system that defines the period (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).
Indicates whether this period is eligible for Same-Store Sales (SSS) or Comparable Store Sales (Comp Sales) analysis. Typically true for periods where stores have been open for at least 12-13 months.
First calendar day of the financial period.
True if the period is used for tax reporting calculations.
Indicates whether this period is a tax reporting period requiring submission of tax returns (e.g., quarterly estimated tax, annual tax filing). Used for tax compliance tracking.
reference: IRS Tax Code, Local Tax Regulations

description: Number of business working days in the period, excluding weekends and holidays. Used for normalizing operational metrics and labor cost analysis.
Concatenated key combining fiscal year and period number (format: YYYYPPP, e.g., 2024001 for FY2024 Period 1). Used for sorting and comparative period analysis.
Unique identifier for the general ledger account record. Primary key.
Reference to the chart of accounts framework this account belongs to. Supports multiple COA structures for different regions or business units.
Reference to the legal entity (company code) to which this GL account belongs. Supports multi-entity consolidation across franchise and company-owned operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Company Code; GAAP/IFRS Consolidation Requirements

description: Reference to the parent account in the account hierarchy for roll-up reporting. Null for top-level accounts. Enables multi-level account group hierarchies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Account Hierarchy; GAAP/IFRS Financial Statement Aggregation

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for accounts that manage balances in a specific currency (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Null for accounts managed only in company code currency.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Currency Management

description: Detailed business description of the account purpose, usage guidelines, and what types of transactions should be posted to this account.
SAP account group code that defines the account's characteristics, field status, and number range. Controls account behavior and validation rules in SAP S/4HANA.
Human-readable short name or title of the GL account (e.g., 'Food Cost - Beef', 'Franchise Royalty Revenue', 'Depreciation - Equipment').
The externally-known unique account code used across all legal entities in the chart of accounts. Typically 6-10 digit numeric code following SAP S/4HANA GL numbering convention.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Chart of Accounts; GAAP/IFRS Account Structure

description: Current lifecycle status of the GL account: 'active' for normal use, 'blocked' for temporarily suspended, 'closed' for permanently retired, 'pending_activation' for newly created accounts awaiting approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA GL Master Data Lifecycle

description: High-level classification of the account into one of the five fundamental financial statement categories: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense.
Alternative or legacy account code used for cross-reference, migration mapping, or external reporting (e.g., mapping to previous ERP system account numbers).
reference: Data Migration Standards; Legacy System Integration

description: Specific balance sheet line item classification for asset, liability, and equity accounts. Set to 'not_applicable' for P&L (Profit and Loss) accounts (revenue/expense).

Classification of this account for cash flow statement presentation: 'operating' for operational cash flows, 'investing' for CapEx (Capital Expenditure) and asset transactions, 'financing' for debt and equity, 'not_applicable' for non-cash accounts.
Mapped account number used in corporate consolidation reporting to standardize accounts across multiple legal entities and chart of accounts structures.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Consolidation Standards (ASC 810 / IFRS 10); SAP S/4HANA Consolidation

description: Classification of cost element: 'primary' for costs posted from external transactions (FI to CO), 'secondary' for internal allocations and assessments, 'not_applicable' if not a cost element.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Element Categories

description: Flag indicating whether this GL account is also defined as a cost element in SAP Controlling (CO) module for cost center and internal order postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) Cost Element Master

description: User ID or name of the person who created this GL account record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA User Audit; SOX Compliance

description: Date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system. Audit trail for account master data governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; SOX Compliance

description: SAP field status group code that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting transactions to this account.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Field Status Variant Configuration

description: Detailed sub-classification within the account type for financial statement presentation (e.g., 'Current Assets', 'Long-Term Liabilities', 'Operating Revenue', 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)', 'Selling General and Administrative (SG&A)').
reference: GAAP/IFRS Financial Statement Presentation; SAP S/4HANA Financial Statement Version

description: Business function or operational area this account supports (e.g., 'Restaurant Operations', 'Franchise', 'Supply Chain', 'Marketing', 'Corporate', 'Real Estate'). Used for segment reporting and management accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Functional Area; GAAP/IFRS Segment Reporting (ASC 280 / IFRS 8)

business_glossary_term: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) Account Indicator
description: Flag indicating whether this account is used in GAAP-compliant financial statements. Supports dual-ledger reporting for companies reporting under both GAAP and IFRS.

business_glossary_term: International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) Account Indicator
description: Flag indicating whether this account is used in IFRS-compliant financial statements. Supports dual-ledger reporting for companies reporting under both GAAP and IFRS.
Flag indicating whether this account is used for intercompany transactions between legal entities that require elimination during consolidation.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this GL account record. Supports change tracking and accountability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA User Audit; SOX Compliance

description: Date and time when this GL account record was last updated. Tracks changes to account master data for audit and compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; SOX Compliance

description: Flag indicating whether individual line item details are stored and displayed for this account. True for most operational accounts; false for summary accounts.
Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or business context about this GL account (e.g., usage guidelines, approval requirements, historical context).
Flag indicating whether this account uses open item management for tracking and clearing individual transactions (e.g., for clearing accounts, suspense accounts).
Granularity level at which budget planning and forecasting is performed for this account (e.g., 'corporate', 'region', 'restaurant', 'cost_center').
reference: SAP S/4HANA Budget Planning; Management Accounting Best Practices

description: Flag indicating whether direct transaction postings are allowed to this account. False for summary/header accounts that only receive totals from sub-accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA GL Account Master Field Status

description: Specific P&L statement line item classification for revenue and expense accounts (e.g., 'Sales Revenue', 'COGS (Cost of Goods Sold)', 'Labor Cost', 'Occupancy Cost', 'EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization)'). Null for balance sheet accounts.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Income Statement Structure; Restaurant Industry P&L Format

description: Flag indicating whether this is a reconciliation account (subledger control account) that summarizes detailed transactions from AP (Accounts Payable), AR (Accounts Receivable), or FA (Fixed Assets) subledgers.
Flag indicating whether transactions posted to this account are included in segment reporting for business unit, geographic, or product line analysis.
SAP sort key that determines the default sorting and grouping of line items posted to this account (e.g., by posting date, document number, or business partner).
reference: SAP S/4HANA Line Item Display Configuration

description: Flag indicating whether this is a statistical account used for tracking non-monetary quantities (e.g., headcount, transaction counts, cover counts) without affecting financial balances.
Tax treatment classification for this account (e.g., 'taxable_revenue', 'non_taxable_revenue', 'input_tax', 'output_tax', 'exempt'). Used for sales tax, VAT, and GST reporting.
reference: IRS Tax Code; State/Local Sales Tax Regulations; SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration

description: Date from which this GL account becomes active and available for transaction posting. Supports time-dependent account master data.
Date until which this GL account remains active. Null for accounts with no planned end date. Used for account retirement and historical reporting.
Primary key for hierarchy_node
Self-referencing FK on hierarchy_node (parent_hierarchy_node_id)
Identifier of the immediate parent node in the hierarchy.
Name of the business unit to which the node belongs.
Financial cost‑center code associated with the node for budgeting and reporting.
Timestamp when the hierarchy node record was first created.
Optional free‑text description providing additional context about the node.
Date when the node becomes effective in the hierarchy.
Date when the node ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.
Identifier of the node in an external system (e.g., SAP hierarchy ID).
Numeric depth of the node within the hierarchy (root = 0).
Delimited path representing the node's ancestry (e.g., /1/4/23).
Indicates whether the node has no child nodes.
Unique business code identifying the node within its hierarchy.
Human‑readable name of the hierarchy node.
Classification of the node indicating its role in the hierarchy (e.g., organization, region, brand).
Monetary or quantitative value associated with the node (e.g., budget allocation).
Numeric weight assigned to the node for allocation or scoring purposes.
Financial profit‑center code linked to the node for performance measurement.
Standardized code representing the geographic region of the node.
Integer used to order sibling nodes under the same parent.
Current lifecycle status of the node.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the hierarchy node record.
Unique identifier for the intercompany transaction record. Primary key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Intercompany Posting Standard

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Replace string GL account references with FKs to gl_account for intercompany transaction clarity.

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Intercompany transactions belong to sending and receiving legal entities; replace string codes with proper FK to legal_entity for referential integrity.
The cost center code to which the intercompany transaction is allocated for management accounting and internal reporting purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Standard

description: The SAP user ID of the person or system account that created the intercompany transaction record in SAP S/4HANA.
The date and time when the intercompany transaction record was first created in SAP S/4HANA. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The date on the source document or invoice that originated the intercompany transaction. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Date Standard

description: The SAP financial document number assigned to the intercompany posting. Ten-digit numeric identifier unique within company code and fiscal year.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Number Range

description: The date by which the intercompany transaction amount is due for settlement between entities. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Due Date Calculation

description: The date on which the intercompany transaction was eliminated during the consolidation process. Null if not yet eliminated. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Boolean flag indicating whether this intercompany transaction has been eliminated in the consolidated financial statements. True if eliminated, False if not yet eliminated.
The foreign exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to group currency at the time of posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Exchange Rate Standard

description: The fiscal period (month or posting period) within the fiscal year in which the transaction is recorded. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods.
The fiscal year in which the intercompany transaction is recorded, per the company's financial calendar.
The transaction amount converted to the group reporting currency for consolidated financial statements and intercompany elimination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Group Currency Standard

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the group reporting currency used for consolidation (typically USD for Restaurants).
The transaction amount converted to the local currency of the sending company code for local statutory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Translation Standard

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the local currency of the sending company code.
The SAP user ID of the person or system account that last modified the intercompany transaction record.
The date and time when the intercompany transaction record was last modified in SAP S/4HANA. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Boolean flag indicating whether this transaction is part of an intercompany netting arrangement where offsetting balances are settled periodically. True if netting applies, False otherwise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Intercompany Netting Process

description: The payment terms agreed between the sending and receiving entities for settlement of the intercompany transaction (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).
The date on which the intercompany transaction was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Date Standard

description: The profit center code associated with the intercompany transaction for segment reporting and profitability analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center Standard

description: The fiscal period (YYYY-MM format) in which the intercompany transaction is scheduled for or has completed reconciliation.
Status indicating whether the intercompany transaction has been successfully reconciled between the sending and receiving entities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Intercompany Reconciliation Process

description: External reference number from the originating system or invoice that supports the intercompany transaction (e.g., purchase order number, invoice number).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reference Field Standard

description: The SAP financial document number of the reversal posting, if this transaction has been reversed. Null if not reversed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Document Standard

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this intercompany transaction has been reversed. True if reversed, False otherwise.
Code indicating the business reason for reversing the intercompany transaction (e.g., error correction, duplicate posting, pricing adjustment). Null if not reversed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Reason Configuration

description: The actual date on which the intercompany transaction was settled or paid. Null if not yet settled. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Settlement Process

description: The gross monetary value of the intercompany transaction in the transaction currency, before any adjustments or eliminations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Amount Field Standard

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the intercompany transaction was originally denominated.
Free-text description providing additional context and details about the nature and purpose of the intercompany transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Text Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the intercompany transaction indicating its processing and reconciliation state.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Status Framework

description: Classification of the intercompany transaction indicating the nature of the charge or transfer between legal entities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Intercompany Transaction Classification

description: Unique identifier for the journal entry record. Primary key for the journal entry header in SAP S/4HANA FI-GL.
SAP user ID of the person who approved this journal entry for posting. Populated only for entries requiring approval workflow. Used for segregation of duties compliance.
SAP user ID of the person who approved this journal entry for posting. Populated only for entries requiring approval workflow. Used for segregation of duties compliance.
SAP user ID of the person or system account that posted this journal entry. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties monitoring.
reference: SOX Section 404 User Access Controls; SAP Security Standards

description: SAP user ID of the person who last modified this journal entry header (e.g., added notes, changed status). Blank if never modified after initial posting.
SAP user ID of the person or system account that posted this journal entry. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties monitoring.
reference: SOX Section 404 User Access Controls; SAP Security Standards

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry was posted in a special adjustment period (periods 13-16) for year-end close. True if adjustment period; False if regular period.
Exact date and time when the journal entry was approved. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if no approval required or not yet approved.

Classification of the journal entry for audit sampling and risk assessment. Values: standard (routine posting), high_risk (unusual or large amount), manual_adjustment (manual journal entry requiring scrutiny), system_generated (automated posting).
reference: SOX Section 404 Risk Assessment; External Audit Standards

description: The baseline date from which payment terms are calculated (typically document date or posting date). Used to determine net due date and discount periods. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Name of the batch input session if this journal entry was posted via batch processing (e.g., mass upload, automated interface). Blank for manually posted entries.
The date on which this journal entry was cleared. Populated only for cleared documents. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
The document number of the clearing document if this journal entry has been cleared (e.g., open item cleared by payment or offsetting entry). Blank if not cleared.

Four-character alphanumeric code identifying the legal entity within the SAP organizational structure. Represents the smallest organizational unit for which a complete self-contained set of accounts can be drawn up for purposes of external reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; GAAP/IFRS Consolidation Requirements

description: Three-character code classifying the journal entry for consolidation purposes (e.g., 100=standard posting, 200=intercompany elimination, 300=currency translation adjustment, 400=consolidation adjustment). Used in multi-entity financial consolidation.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the document currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All line item amounts in this journal entry are denominated in this currency.
The date printed on the source document (invoice date, receipt date, contract date). May differ from posting date. Used for aging analysis and payment terms calculation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Free-text description or memo entered at the document header level. Provides business context for the journal entry (e.g., 'Monthly depreciation run', 'Accrual for Q4 marketing expenses').

Ten-digit accounting document number assigned by SAP upon posting. The externally-known unique identifier for this journal entry within the company code and fiscal year. Used for audit trail and cross-reference.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Management; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: Two-character code classifying the nature of the accounting document (e.g., SA=GL account document, KR=vendor invoice, DR=customer invoice, AB=accounting document, AA=asset posting). Controls number ranges and posting keys.
The calendar date on which the document was entered into the SAP system by the user. Used for audit trail and processing time analysis. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
The exchange rate applied to convert document currency to local currency at the time of posting. Expressed as local currency per one unit of document currency.
Numeric posting period within the fiscal year (1-12 for regular periods, 13-16 for special/adjustment periods). Used for monthly P&L reporting and period-end close.
Four-digit fiscal year in which the document is posted. Determines the accounting period structure and year-end closing scope.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry involves intercompany transactions between multiple legal entities within the corporate group. True if intercompany; False otherwise.
Exact date and time of the last modification to this journal entry header. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if never modified.

Two-character code identifying the ledger in which this entry is posted (e.g., 0L=leading ledger for GAAP, 2L=IFRS ledger, 3L=management ledger). Supports parallel accounting for multiple reporting standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA New GL; GAAP/IFRS Parallel Accounting

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company code local currency. Used for statutory reporting and consolidation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP/IFRS Functional Currency

description: The calculated due date for payment based on payment terms and baseline date. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP/AR aging. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is in parked status (saved but not yet posted). True if parked; False if posted. Parked documents do not update GL balances until posted.
Four-character code defining payment terms for vendor invoices or customer invoices (e.g., net 30, 2/10 net 30). Used to calculate due dates and cash discount eligibility.
The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the primary date for financial reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Posting Logic

description: Two-digit numeric code that controls the type of posting (debit or credit) and the account type (e.g., 40=debit GL, 50=credit GL, 01=debit customer, 31=debit vendor). Determines field status and processing logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Key Configuration

description: Exact date and time when the journal entry was posted to the general ledger. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and real-time reporting.
Free-text field for external reference numbers such as vendor invoice number, purchase order number, or intercompany document reference. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a prior posting. True if this document reverses another document; False otherwise.
